functions.name: CompileDate
functions.description: Returns the date the plug-in was compiled. Useful for determining if when the currently installed plug-in was released. See Plugin.CompileDate to get a FileMaker date instead of a text. Format of this text is "mmm DD YYYY" with month as three letter text in English.

examples.label: Get the Compile Date and Time:
examples.text_code:
$r
=
MBS
("CompileDate") & " " &
MBS
("CompileTime")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CompileDate") & " " & MBS("CompileTime")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Get year:
examples.text_code:
Let([
year = GetAsNumber(Middle(
MBS
( "CompileDate" );7;6))
]; year)
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
 year = GetAsNumber(Middle(MBS( "CompileDate" );7;6))
]; year)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Remind to update plugin:
examples.text_code:
If [ GetAsNumber(Middle(
MBS
( "CompileDate" );7;6)) < Year ( Get(CurrentDate )) and Month ( Get(CurrentDate )) > 3 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Your
MBS
Plugin could be updated”. ; "It's spring and you use plugin from last year?" ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ GetAsNumber(Middle(MBS( "CompileDate" );7;6)) < Year ( Get(CurrentDate )) and Month ( Get(CurrentDate )) > 3 ] 
	Show Custom Dialog [ "Your MBS Plugin could be updated”. ; "It's spring and you use plugin from last year?" ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CUPS.CancelJob
functions.description: Cancel a print job on the default server.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Name of the printer.

parameters.name: JobID
parameters.description: The job number of the job you want to cancel. Pass -1 to cancel all jobs or 0 to cancel current job.

examples.label: Cancel all jobs on this printer queue:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "CUPS.CancelJob"; "Brother_DCP_8085DN"; -1 )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CUPS.CancelJob"; "Brother_DCP_8085DN"; -1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CUPS.GetDefaultPrinterName
functions.description: Get the default printer or class for the default server. This function returns the default printer or class as defined by the LPDEST or PRINTER environment variables. If these environment variables are not set, the server default destination is returned. Returns empty string if no default printer is defined.
functions.result: The name of the default printer.

examples.label: Query default printer name:
examples.text_code:
$result
=
MBS
( "CUPS.GetDefaultPrinterName" )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CUPS.GetDefaultPrinterName" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CUPS.GetJobsAsJSON
functions.description: Queries some jobs and returns JSON Array.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Name of the printer queue to query. If no name or empty string is specified, we get all queues.

parameters.name: OnlyMyJobs
parameters.description: Pass either "my" for your users jobs or "all" for all jobs.

parameters.name: whichJobs
parameters.description: Pass "active" to get only active jobs, "all" for all jobs or "complete" for completed jobs.

examples.label: Find all jobs:
examples.text_code:
MBS
("CUPS.GetJobsAsJSON"; ""; "all"; "all")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CUPS.GetJobsAsJSON"; ""; "all"; "all")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CUPS.LastErrorCode
functions.description: Return the last IPP status code.

examples.label: This cancels a job and queries last error code (will be 1030) and error message (something like "job 123 doesn't exist"):
examples.text_code:
$result
=
MBS
( "
CUPS.CancelJob
"; "test"; 123 )
$message
=
MBS
( "
CUPS.LastErrorMessage
" )
$errorCode
=
MBS
( "CUPS.LastErrorCode" )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CUPS.CancelJob"; "test"; 123 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CUPS.LastErrorMessage" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$message</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CUPS.LastErrorCode" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$errorCode</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CUPS.PrintData
functions.description: Print a file to a printer or class on the default server. With \xx you can embed any character in the text. \t gives a tab, \n a LF, \r a newline.

parameters.name: Printer
parameters.description: The name of the printer. Please use CUPS printer name with underscore instead of spaces.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: The file data to print. You can include escapes with backslash. \n gives new line, \r gives return, \\ gives a backslash. \xx with a hex number gives that character. e.g. \41 gives an A.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: The title of the print job.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: List of options. Simply add key and values as string parameters to the function call.

examples.label: Print data:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "CUPS.PrintData"; "Brother_DCP_8085DN"; "Hello World\nJust a test!\n"; "FileMaker Printout" )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CUPS.PrintData"; "Brother_DCP_8085DN"; "Hello World\nJust a test!\n"; "FileMaker Printout" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Print data with options:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CUPS.PrintData"; "Brother_DCP_8085DN"; "Hello World\nJust a test!\n"; "FileMaker Printout"; 
// first option
"Scaling"; "75";
// second option
"media"; "A4" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Build Data, send to printer and write a text file for debugging:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$data
; Value:"#!A1#DC					REM Drucker wird aktiviert¶" & "#IMSR103
.87
/
25.00
REM Etikettenmasse¶" & "#PR5/5/					REM Druckgeschwindigkeit¶" & "#HV70					REM Druckhitze¶" & "#ER¶" & "¶" & "¶" & "¶" & "#T06
.5
#J18
.7
#YN101/1R/33///\\80#G¶" …]
Set Variable [
$data
; Value:
MBS
( "
Text.ReplaceNewLine
";
$data
; 3 )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Text.WriteTextFile
";
$data
; "/tmp/test.txt"; "Dos" )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "CUPS.PrintData"; "LabelPrinter";
$data
; "Label" )]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"#!A1#DC					REM Drucker wird aktiviert¶" & "#IMSR103.87/25.00		REM Etikettenmasse¶" & "#PR5/5/					REM Druckgeschwindigkeit¶" & "#HV70					REM Druckhitze¶" & "#ER¶" & "¶" & "¶" & "¶" & "#T06.5 #J18.7 #YN101/1R/33///\\80#G¶" …</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$data</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.ReplaceNewline"; $data; 3 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$data</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.WriteTextFile"; $data; "/tmp/test.txt"; "Dos" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CUPS.PrintData"; "LabelPrinter"; $data; "Label" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CUPS.PrintDataEncoding
functions.signature: MBS( "CUPS.PrintDataEncoding"; Printer; Data; Encoding { ; Title; Options } )
functions.description: Print a file to a printer or class on the default server. With \xx you can embed any character in the text. \t gives a tab, \n a LF, \r a newline.

parameters.name: Printer
parameters.description: The name of the printer. Please use CUPS printer name with underscore instead of spaces.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: The file data to print. You can include escapes with backslash. \n gives new line, \r gives return, \\ gives a backslash. \xx with a hex number gives that character. e.g. \41 gives an A.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for data. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: The title of the print job.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: List of options. Simply add key and values as string parameters to the function call.

examples.label: Sends some data in Windows encoding:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$data
; Value:"Hello World.¶Tägliche Grüße!"]
Set Variable [
$data
; Value:
MBS
( "
Text.ReplaceNewLine
";
$data
; 3 )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Text.WriteTextFile
";
$data
; "/tmp/test.txt"; "Windows" )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "CUPS.PrintDataEncoding"; "Brother_DCP_8085DN";
$data
; "Windows"; "Test Print" )]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Hello World.¶Tägliche Grüße!"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$data</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.ReplaceNewline"; $data; 3 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$data</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.WriteTextFile"; $data; "/tmp/test.txt"; "Windows" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CUPS.PrintDataEncoding"; "Brother_DCP_8085DN"; $data; "Windows"; "Test Print" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CUPS.PrintFile
functions.description: Print a file to a printer or class on the default server.

parameters.name: Printer
parameters.description: The name of the printer. Please use CUPS printer name with underscore instead of spaces.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The file path of the file to print.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: The title of the print job.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: List of options. Simply add key and values as string parameters to the function call.

examples.label: Print a file:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "CUPS.PrintFile"; "Brother_DCP_8085DN"; "/test/testfile.txt"; "FileMaker Printout" )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

functions.name: Dictionary.AddDictionary
functions.description: Adds entries from other dictionary to current one.

parameters.name: DictionaryRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the destination dictionary.

parameters.name: OtherDictionary
parameters.description: The reference number to the source dictionary.

functions.name: Dictionary.AddSQL
functions.signature: MBS( "Dictionary.AddSQL"; DictionaryRef; SQL Statement; FileName { ; Params... } )
functions.description: Adds values to a dictionary via SQL. From the result set, the first field is used as key and second field if exists as value. Returns error in case of SQL error with error message. You don't need to use ORDER BY in SQL as the order in a dictionary is defined by storage which for you means unordered. This function is currently only supported for FileMaker 11 and newer. If you need it for older versions, please contact us. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Params parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: DictionaryRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the dictionary.

parameters.name: SQL Statement
parameters.description: SQL Statement as a Text string

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The target database name. Can be empty to not limit query to one database.

parameters.name: Params...
parameters.description: Pass here parameters for SQL. One parameter to this function for each parameter you want to pass to the SQL statement.

functions.result: Returns number of items added or error.

examples.label: Test function:
examples.text_code:
# create dictionary
Set Variable [
$dic
; Value:
MBS
("
Dictionary.Create
") ]
# add keys and values via SQL
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Dictionary.AddSQL";
$dic
; "SELECT TextField, NumberField FROM \"Test Table\"") ]
# query keys
Set Variable [
$keys
; Value:
MBS
("
Dictionary.Keys
";
$dic
) ]
# and values
Set Variable [
$values
; Value:
MBS
("
Dictionary.ValueForKey
";
$dic
; "Hello") ]
# free
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Dictionary.Release
";
$dic
) ]

      <Calculation>MBS("Dictionary.Create") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$dic</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> add keys and values via SQL</Text>
  </Step>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Dictionary.AddSQL"; $dic; "SELECT TextField, NumberField FROM \"Test Table\"") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> query keys</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Dictionary.Keys"; $dic) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$keys</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and values</Text>
  </Step>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Dictionary.ValueForKey"; $dic; "Hello") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$values</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Dictionary.AddSQLRecord
functions.signature: MBS( "Dictionary.AddSQLRecord"; DictionaryRef; Keys; SQL Statement; FileName { ; Params... } )
functions.description: Adds values from a record to dictionary via SQL. From the result set, the first record is used for values and given key list for the keys. Returns error in case of SQL error with error message. As we only use first record, you can use FETCH FIRST 1 ROWS ONLY in the SQL command to only query first record. This function is currently only supported for FileMaker 11 and newer. If you need it for older versions, please contact us. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Params parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: DictionaryRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the dictionary.

parameters.name: Keys
parameters.description: The list of keys for the dictionary. Could be the same as the field list.

parameters.name: SQL Statement
parameters.description: SQL Statement as a Text string

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The target database name. Can be empty to not limit query to one database.

parameters.name: Params...
parameters.description: Pass here parameters. One parameter to this function for each parameter you want to pass to the SQL statement.

Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Params parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: Params...

functions.name: Dictionary.Create
functions.description: Creates a new dictionary. Dictionaries are hash maps, so lookup is very fast (often faster than search in your FileMaker database). You can use them for storing parameters or lookup tables. Optionally you can pass keys and values as parameters to this function. If you looked for associative arrays, you are right here. Dictionaries can have multiple million of entries without problems. Just be aware of FileMaker's standard loop/recursion limit of 50000, which can be raised with SetRecursion function. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Value parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: Optional, a key.

parameters.name: Value...
parameters.description: Optional, a value.

functions.result: Returns dictionary reference number or error.

examples.label: Build a dictionary in a calculation:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
/*
Create new Dictionary
*/
	param = MBS( "Dictionary.Create" );

/*
	Fill in parameters
*/ 
	r1 = MBS( "Dictionary.SetValueForKey"; param; "text"; Pass Parameters with Dictionary::TextField );
	r2 = MBS( "Dictionary.SetValueForKey"; param; "number"; Pass Parameters with Dictionary::NumberField);
	r3 = MBS( "Dictionary.SetValueForKey"; param; "date"; Pass Parameters with Dictionary::DateField);
	r4 = MBS( "Dictionary.SetValueForKey"; param; "time"; Pass Parameters with Dictionary::TimeField);
	r5 = MBS( "Dictionary.SetValueForKey"; param; "timestamp"; Pass Parameters with Dictionary::TimeStampField);
	r6 = MBS( "Dictionary.SetValueForKey"; param; "container"; Pass Parameters with Dictionary::ContainerField)
/*
	return dictionary
*/
 ] ; param )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Test script to look for duplicate UUIDs:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$dic
; Value:
MBS
( "Dictionary.Create") ]
Loop
Set Variable [
$uuid
; Value: Get(UUID) ]
If [
MBS
("
Dictionary.HasKey
";
$dic
;
$uuid
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Duplicate!" ;
$uuid
]
Exit Loop If [ 1 ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Dictionary.SetValueForKey
";
$dic
;
$uuid
;
$uuid
) ]
End Loop
Set Field [
test::test
;
MBS
( "
Dictionary.Keys
";
$dic
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Dictionary.Release
";
$dic
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Dictionary.Create") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$dic</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(UUID) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$uuid</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("Dictionary.HasKey"; $dic; $uuid) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Duplicate!" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$uuid </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> 1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Dictionary.SetValueForKey"; $dic; $uuid; $uuid ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "Dictionary.Keys"; $dic ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="test" id="99999" name="test "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Dictionary.Release"; $dic ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

CF
; Value:

$FunctionName
; Value: "Greet" ]
Set Variable [
$Body
; Value: "/// Name¶¶\"Welcome \" & Name & \"!\"" ]

$r
; Value:

( "

"; "test";
$FunctionName
;
$Body

; Value:
MBS
("FM.
CF
"; "test"; "Greet"; "Peter") ]

$r
]
#

$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Dictionary.Release
"; "test") ]

functions.name: Dictionary.Deserialize
functions.description: Restores a serialized dictionary.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The serialized data as text.

functions.result: Returns dictionary reference number or error.

examples.label: Create a dictionary and serialize it:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:
MBS
( "
Dictionary.Create
"; "Hello"; "World"; "1234"; "Just a test!")]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
MBS
("
Dictionary.Serialize
";
$list
)]
Set Variable [
$SecondList
; Value:
MBS
("Dictionary.Deserialize";
$text
)]
Show Custom Dialog ["List"; "Encoded: " &
$text
& ¶ & ¶ &  "Decoded Keys: " &
MBS
("
Dictionary.Keys
";
$secondList

examples.label: Serialize and unserialize with date and time:
examples.text_code:
# Create Dictionary
Set Variable [
$dict
; Value:
MBS
( "
Dictionary.Create
" )]
# put date and time inside:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Dictionary.SetValueForKey
";
$dict
; "theTime"; Get ( CurrentTime ))]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Dictionary.SetValueForKey
";
$dict
; "theDate"; Get ( CurrentDate ))]
# Serialize this
Set Variable [
$dictEncoded
; Value:
MBS
( "
Dictionary.Serialize
";
$dict
)]
#Free dictionary
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Dictionary.Release
";
$dict
)]
# Unpack it:
Set Variable [
$newDict
; Value:
MBS
( "Dictionary.Deserialize";
$dictEncoded
)]
# and get values
Set Variable [
$TheTime
; Value:
MBS
( "
Dictionary.ValueForKey
";
$newDict
; "theTime" )]
Set Variable [
$TheDate
; Value:
MBS
( "
Dictionary.ValueForKey
";
$newDict
; "theDate" )]
Show Custom Dialog [
$TheTime
& " " &
$TheDate
]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Dictionary.Release
";
$newDict
)]

functions.name: Dictionary.FromJSON
functions.description: Creates dictionary from JSON. Numbers and text are passed. Booleans become number. NULL will be empty text. Objects and arrays are passed as JSON as text.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

functions.result: Returns dictionary reference number or error.

examples.label: Create from dictionary:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Dictionary.FromJSON"; "{\"Hello\": \"World\"}" )

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Create dictionary from JSON and return it back:

( "
Dictionary.ToJSON
";
MBS
( "Dictionary.FromJSON";
"{
\"Hello\": \"World\",
\"num\": 123,
\"value\":
123.456
,
\"object\": {\"Hello\": \"World\"},
\"empty\": null,
\"array\": [1,2,3]}"))

functions.name: Dictionary.Keys

parameters.name: DictionaryRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the dictionary.

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Dictionary.KeysWithPostfix
functions.description: Returns list with all keys matching postfix. The order is random and defined by how the dictionary internally stores values. See also Dictionary.Keys . The postfix and prefix keys functions allows you to organize things in the dictionaries with e.g. dot notation. You may have entries like "person.123.firstname" and "person.123.lastname" and by using Dictionary.KeysWithPostfix you can look for ".firstname" to find all keys ending with this postfix.

parameters.name: DictionaryRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the dictionary.

parameters.name: Postfix
parameters.description: The postfix text

examples.label: Try it:
examples.text_code:
Let([
// create a dicitonary
dic =
MBS
( "
Dictionary.Create
");
// fill in some values:
t =
MBS
( "
Dictionary.SetValueForKey
"; dic; "person
.123
.firstname"; "John" );
t =
MBS
( "
Dictionary.SetValueForKey
"; dic; "person
.123
.lastname"; "Miller" );
t =
MBS
( "
Dictionary.SetValueForKey
"; dic; "person
.456
.firstname"; "Tom" );
t =
MBS
( "
Dictionary.SetValueForKey
"; dic; "person
.456
.lastname"; "Smith" );
// and query keys:
r =
MBS
( "Dictionary.KeysWithPostfix"; dic; ".lastname" )
]; r)
Example result:
person.456.lastname
person.123.lastname
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
// create a dicitonary
dic = MBS( "Dictionary.Create");
// fill in some values:
t = MBS( "Dictionary.SetValueForKey"; dic; "person.123.firstname"; "John" );
t = MBS( "Dictionary.SetValueForKey"; dic; "person.123.lastname"; "Miller" );
t = MBS( "Dictionary.SetValueForKey"; dic; "person.456.firstname"; "Tom" );
t = MBS( "Dictionary.SetValueForKey"; dic; "person.456.lastname"; "Smith" );
// and query keys:
r = MBS( "Dictionary.KeysWithPostfix"; dic; ".lastname" )
]; r)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Dictionary.KeysWithPrefix
functions.description: Returns list with all keys matching prefix. The order is random and defined by how the dictionary internally stores values. See also Dictionary.Keys . The postfix and prefix keys functions allows you to organize things in the dictionaries with e.g. dot notation. You may have entries like "person.123.firstname" and "person.123.lastname" and by using Dictionary.KeysWithPrefix you can look for "person.123." to find all keys starting with this prefix.

parameters.name: DictionaryRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the dictionary.

parameters.name: Prefix
parameters.description: The prefix text

examples.label: Try it:
examples.text_code:

// create a dicitonary
dic =
MBS
( "
Dictionary.Create
");
// fill in some values:

MBS
( "
Dictionary.SetValueForKey
"; dic; "person
.123
.firstname"; "John" );
t =
MBS
( "
Dictionary.SetValueForKey
"; dic; "person
.123
.lastname"; "Miller" );
t =
MBS
( "
Dictionary.SetValueForKey
"; dic; "person
.456
.firstname"; "Tom" );
t =
MBS
( "
Dictionary.SetValueForKey
"; dic; "person
.456
.lastname"; "Smith" );
// and query keys:
r =
MBS
( "Dictionary.KeysWithPrefix"; dic; "person
.123
." )
]; r)
Example result:
person.123.lastname
person.123.firstname
examples.xml_code:

// create a dicitonary
dic = MBS( "Dictionary.Create");
// fill in some values:

functions.name: Dictionary.Lookup
functions.description: Looks up value for the key. As dictionary preserves data type, result can be empty, text, number, date, time, timestamp, container or boolean. Returns default value (or empty result), if key is not found. Similar to Dictionary.ValueForKey , but without an error, but a default value.

parameters.name: DictionaryRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the dictionary.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The key to use. Keys are case sensitive.

parameters.name: DefaultValue
parameters.description: The default value to return if key is not found. Default for the default value is an empty string.

functions.name: Dictionary.SetList
functions.description: Fills dictionary with keys and values form a list. The list contains key=value entries with a configurable delimiter.

parameters.name: DictionaryRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the dictionary.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list of values with delimiter.

parameters.name: Delimiter
parameters.description: The delimiter for the value list. Default is tab key.

functions.name: Dictionary.SetQuickList
functions.description: Fills dictionary with keys and values form a quick list. The list contains key=value entries with a configurable delimiter.

parameters.name: DictionaryRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the dictionary.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Delimiter
parameters.description: The delimiter for the value list. Default is tab key.

functions.name: Dictionary.SetTag
functions.description: Sets the tag value. The tag value allows you to store any value related to the dictionary here like a RecordID in FileMaker.

parameters.name: DictionaryRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the dictionary.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new tag value.

functions.name: Dictionary.SetValueForKey
functions.description: Stores value for given key. Data type is preserved, so you can even store containers in the dictionary. Keys should be text and are handled case sensitive.

parameters.name: DictionaryRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the dictionary.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The key to use. Keys are case sensitive. Also keys must be text and should be single line.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new value. Supported data types include empty, text, number, boolean, container, time, timestamp and date.

examples.label: Build a dictionary in a calculation:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
/*
Create new Dictionary
*/
	param = MBS( "Dictionary.Create" );

/*
	Fill in parameters
*/ 
	r1 = MBS( "Dictionary.SetValueForKey"; param; "text"; Pass Parameters with Dictionary::TextField );
	r2 = MBS( "Dictionary.SetValueForKey"; param; "number"; Pass Parameters with Dictionary::NumberField);
	r3 = MBS( "Dictionary.SetValueForKey"; param; "date"; Pass Parameters with Dictionary::DateField);
	r4 = MBS( "Dictionary.SetValueForKey"; param; "time"; Pass Parameters with Dictionary::TimeField);
	r5 = MBS( "Dictionary.SetValueForKey"; param; "timestamp"; Pass Parameters with Dictionary::TimeStampField);
	r6 = MBS( "Dictionary.SetValueForKey"; param; "container"; Pass Parameters with Dictionary::ContainerField)
/*
	return dictionary
*/
 ] ; param )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Dictionary.SetVariables
functions.description: Creates variables with given keys and values from dictionary. The plugin evaluates a Let statement which defines variables in current script with the content of the dictionary. This is very useful for passing arguments to a script (see blog post & guide) This does not work, if passing dictionary reference number from client to server or back!

parameters.name: DictionaryRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the dictionary.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: Whether to create local or global variables. Pass 2 to create global variables ($$) Pass 0 to create local variables ($), default. Add 1 to free the dictionary after doing this.

functions.name: Dictionary.ToJSON
functions.description: Returns JSON representation of dictionary for data exchange. This is useful for passing data from dictionary to some javascript.

parameters.name: DictionaryRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the dictionary.

functions.name: Dictionary.ToText
functions.signature: MBS( "Dictionary.ToText"; DictionaryRef { ; Column Delimiter; Row Delimiter } )
functions.description: Returns text representation of dictionary for data exchange. Containers are just added as text and delimiters inside a text value can cause trouble.

parameters.name: DictionaryRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the dictionary.

parameters.name: Column Delimiter
parameters.description: The delimiter between key and value. Should not be part of content. By default we use tab key.

parameters.name: Row Delimiter
parameters.description: The delimiter between rows. By default we use return key (char 13)

functions.name: Dictionary.ToXML
functions.description: Returns XML representation of dictionary for data exchange.

parameters.name: DictionaryRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the dictionary.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: If 1, we format pretty. Pass 0 to get compact xml.

parameters.name: RootName
parameters.description: The root name for the XML. Default is dictionary.

functions.name: Dictionary.ValueForKey
functions.description: Queries value for the key. As dictionary preserves data type, result can be empty, text, number, date, time, timestamp, container or boolean. Returns an error, if key is not found. See Dictionary.Lookup to pass default value.

parameters.name: DictionaryRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the dictionary.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The key to use. Keys are case sensitive.

examples.label: Serialize and unserialize with date and time:
examples.text_code:
# Create Dictionary
Set Variable [
$dict
; Value:
MBS
( "
Dictionary.Create
" )]
# put date and time inside:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Dictionary.SetValueForKey
";
$dict
; "theTime"; Get ( CurrentTime ))]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Dictionary.SetValueForKey
";
$dict
; "theDate"; Get ( CurrentDate ))]
# Serialize this
Set Variable [
$dictEncoded
; Value:
MBS
( "
Dictionary.Serialize
";
$dict
)]
#Free dictionary
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Dictionary.Release
";
$dict
)]
# Unpack it:
Set Variable [
$newDict
; Value:
MBS
( "
Dictionary.Deserialize
";
$dictEncoded
)]
# and get values
Set Variable [
$TheTime
; Value:
MBS
( "Dictionary.ValueForKey";
$newDict
; "theTime" )]
Set Variable [
$TheDate
; Value:
MBS
( "Dictionary.ValueForKey";
$newDict
; "theDate" )]
Show Custom Dialog [
$TheTime
& " " &
$TheDate
]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Dictionary.Release
";
$newDict
)]

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Serialize this</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ( "Dictionary.Serialize"; $dict )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$dictEncoded</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Free dictionary</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ( "Dictionary.Release"; $dict )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Unpack it:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ( "Dictionary.Deserialize"; $dictEncoded )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$newDict</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and get values</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ( "Dictionary.ValueForKey"; $newDict; "theTime" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$TheTime</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ( "Dictionary.ValueForKey"; $newDict; "theDate" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$TheDate</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>$TheTime & " " & $TheDate</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ( "Dictionary.Release"; $newDict )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Dictionary.ValueTypeForKey
functions.description: Queries data type of the value for the key. Value can be Invalid, Text, Number, Date, Time, TimeStamp, Container or Boolean.

parameters.name: DictionaryRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the dictionary.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The key to use. Keys are case sensitive.

functions.name: Dictionary.Values
functions.description: Returns list with all values as text. Of course data in dictionary is converted to text, so this function will not return details like data type or container content. Order is same as for Dictionary.Keys if the dictionary is not changed inbetween.

parameters.name: DictionaryRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the dictionary.

functions.name: FileDialogWatcher.GetLastPath
functions.description: Queries the last path used in open/save dialogs. This returns native path like "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.jpg". Use Path.NativePathToFileMakerPath to convert to a FileMaker path if you plan to use "filemac:" for an insert file script step. Does not work with FileMaker 8.5, 9 or 10. Works with FileMaker 11 and 12. Added support for Windows with version 10.4, but doesn't work for FileMaker's own dialogs.

functions.name: FileDialogWatcher.Install
functions.description: Installs the required code to catch file names from open/save dialogs on Mac. You need to call this method before FileDialogWatcher.GetLastPath reports something useful. Does not work with FileMaker 8.5, 9 or 10. Works with FileMaker 11 and 12. Added support for Windows with version 10.4, but doesn't work for FileMaker's own dialogs.

functions.name: iOSKeyboard.State
functions.description: Queries keyboard state. State is 0 for hidden, 1 for showing animation, 2 for showing, 3 for hiding animation. This function checks for a license. Created 19th September 2017 , last changed 19th September 2017 iOSKeyboard.SetWillShowEvaluate 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: IsRegistered
functions.description: Queries if plugin is registered with Complete key. See also Register, IsRuntime , IsServer and IsClient .

parameters.name: Component
parameters.description: Which part you want to check.

functions.result: Returns 1 if the plugin is registered or 0 if not.

examples.label: Register only when needed:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
("IsRegistered") ≠ 1]
If [
MBS
("IsRuntime")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Register"; "test"; "Runtime"; 123; 123)]
Else If [
MBS
("IsMobile")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Register"; "test"; "iOS"; 123; 123)]
Else If [
MBS
("IsServer")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Register"; "test"; "Server"; 123; 123)]
Else
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Register"; "test"; "5 seats"; 123; 123)]
End If
End If

	If [MBS("IsRuntime")]

	Else If [MBS("IsMobile")]
		Set Variable [$r; Value:MBS("Register"; "test"; "iOS"; 123; 123)]
	Else If [MBS("IsServer")]
		Set Variable [$r; Value:MBS("Register"; "test"; "Server"; 123; 123)]
	Else
		Set Variable [$r; Value:MBS("Register"; "test"; "5 seats"; 123; 123)]
	End If
End If</Calculation>

    </Repetition>

  </Step>

examples.label: Register with Startup script for server and client:
examples.text_code:
#Startup Script:
#Register for client
Perform Script [“InitMBS”]
#Register for server. Will be ignored if no server or no plugin installed on server
Perform Script on Server [“InitMBS”]
#InitMBS script:
#Enable debug logging. Shows messages in DebugView/Console.app
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Trace")]
#Register if needed for right platform
If [
MBS
("IsRegistered") = 0]
If [
MBS
("IsRuntime")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Register"; "test"; "Complete"; "Runtime"; 123; 123)]
Else If [
MBS
("IsServer")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Register"; "test"; "Complete"; "Server"; 123; 123)]
Else If [
MBS
("IsClient")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Register"; "test"; "Complete"; "5 Seats"; 123; 123)]
Else
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:"Unknown platform: " &
MBS
("Platform")]
End If
#Show errors, so developer can fix them
If [
$r
≠ "OK"]
Show Custom Dialog ["InitMBS failed.";
$r
]
End If
End If
#May initialize other stuff
#like dynapdf with library in extensions folder
If [
MBS
("
DynaPDF.IsInitialized
") = 0]
Set Variable [
$DynaPDFLicense
; Value:"1003637-16022016-3-8-12-685C57F..."]
If [
MBS
("IsWindows")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DynaPDF.Initialize
"; "dynapdf.dll";
$DynaPDFLicense
)]
Else If [
MBS
("IsMacOSX")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DynaPDF.Initialize
"; "dynapdf.dylib";
$DynaPDFLicense
)]
Else
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:"Unknown platform: " &
MBS
("Platform")]
End If
#Show errors, so developer can fix them
If [
$r
≠ "OK"]
Show Custom Dialog ["InitMBS failed.";
$r
]
End If
End If

examples.label: Script to report back status of MBS Plugin:
examples.text_code:
# Script to trigger somewhere to report back details about
MBS
Plugin
Set Variable [
$version
; Value: GetAsText(
MBS
("Version")) ]
Set Variable [
$platform
; Value:
MBS
( "Platform" ) ]
Set Variable [
$buildNumber
; Value:
MBS
( "
Plugin.BuildNumber
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
( "
Plugin.Path
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$IsRegistered
; Value:
MBS
( "IsRegistered" ) ]
Set Variable [
$Name
; Value:
MBS
( "
Plugin.LicenseeName
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$Debug
; Value:
MBS
( "
Plugin.isDebug
" ) ]
If [
$version
= "?" ]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value: "
MBS
Plugin not installed. " & Get ( ApplicationVersion ) ]
Else
Set Variable [
$l1
; Value: "
MBS
Plugin " &
$version
& ", build " &
$buildNumber
& " installed in " & Get ( ApplicationVersion ) ]
Set Variable [
$l2
; Value: "¶Platform: " &
$Platform
& "¶Path: " &
$path
]
Set Variable [
$l3
; Value: If(
$IsRegistered
; "¶Licensed for: " &
$Name
; "¶No license set.") ]
Set Variable [
$l4
; Value: "¶Debug: " & If(
$debug
; "yes"; "no") ]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
$l1
&
$l2
&
$l3
]
End If
Exit Script [ Text Result:
$text
]

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"¶Debug: " & If($debug; "yes"; "no") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$l4</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$l1 & $l2 & $l3 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result: $text </Calculation>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.Add
functions.signature: MBS( "Matrix.Add"; MatrixRef; Value { ; FirstRow; LastRow; firstCol; lastCol } )
functions.description: Adds a value to cells in matrix. We loop over all rows in all columns for the range and add the given value to the numbers. If the cell doesn't contain a number, it's converted to a number like GetAsNumber(). Use Matrix.New or Matrix.NewWithSQL to create the matrix object before adding something to it.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to add to all cells.

parameters.name: FirstRow
parameters.description: The index of first row. Default is 0.

parameters.name: LastRow
parameters.description: The index of last row. Default is Matrix.Height -1.

parameters.name: firstCol
parameters.description: The index of first column. Default is 0.

parameters.name: lastCol
parameters.description: The index of last column. Default is Matrix.Width -1.

examples.label: Add one to each cell:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$matrix
; Value:
MBS
("
Matrix.New
"; 5; 6) ]
# set values
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Matrix.SetRow
";
$matrix
; 0; "Joe¶Miller¶New York¶123") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Matrix.SetRow
";
$matrix
; 1; "Bob¶Jones¶Washington¶234") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Matrix.SetRow
";
$matrix
; 2; "Jane¶Stones¶Los Angeles¶567") ]
# add one to all
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Matrix.Add";
$matrix
; 1; 0; 3; 3; 3) ]
# show numbers
Show Custom Dialog [ "Values" ;
MBS
("
Matrix.GetColumn
";
$matrix
; 3) ]
# free
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Matrix.Release
";
$matrix
) ]
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.New"; 5; 6) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$matrix</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set values</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.SetRow"; $matrix; 0; "Joe¶Miller¶New York¶123") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.SetRow"; $matrix; 1; "Bob¶Jones¶Washington¶234") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.SetRow"; $matrix; 2; "Jane¶Stones¶Los Angeles¶567") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> add one to all</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.Add"; $matrix; 1; 0; 3; 3; 3) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> show numbers</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Values" ; MBS("Matrix.GetColumn"; $matrix</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>3) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> free</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.Release"; $matrix) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.Append
functions.description: Appends second matrix to first one. Source and destination can be the same and then we duplicate all rows on the end. The width of both matrices should be the same, but doesn’t need to be.

parameters.name: Matrix1
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: Matrix2
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

examples.label: Appends a matrix to another:
examples.text_code:
Let([
// new matrix
matrix =
MBS
("
Matrix.New
"; 3; 4);
// add some content
r =
MBS
( "
Matrix.SetRow
"; matrix; 0; "00¶01¶02¶03" );
r =
MBS
( "
Matrix.SetRow
"; matrix; 1; "10¶11¶12¶13" );
r =
MBS
( "
Matrix.SetRow
"; matrix; 2; "20¶21¶22¶23" );
// creates a second matrix
matrix2 =
MBS
("
Matrix.New
"; 1; 4);
r =
MBS
( "
Matrix.SetRow
"; matrix2; 0; "a¶b¶c¶d" );
// copy content of first matrix to second
r =
MBS
( "Matrix.Append"; matrix; matrix2  );
// output matrix to check
csv =
MBS
("
Matrix.CSV
"; matrix)
]; csv)
Example result:
00;01;02;03
10;11;12;13
20;21;22;23
a;b;c;d
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
   // new matrix
   matrix = MBS("Matrix.New"; 3; 4);
   // add some content
   r = MBS( "Matrix.SetRow"; matrix; 0; "00¶01¶02¶03" );
   r = MBS( "Matrix.SetRow"; matrix; 1; "10¶11¶12¶13" );
   r = MBS( "Matrix.SetRow"; matrix; 2; "20¶21¶22¶23" );
   // creates a second matrix
   matrix2 = MBS("Matrix.New"; 1; 4);
   r = MBS( "Matrix.SetRow"; matrix2; 0; "a¶b¶c¶d" );
   // copy content of first matrix to second
   r = MBS( "Matrix.Append"; matrix; matrix2  );
   // output matrix to check
   csv = MBS("Matrix.CSV"; matrix)
]; csv)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.CSV
functions.signature: MBS( "Matrix.CSV"; MatrixRef { ; FirstRow; LastRow; firstCol; lastCol; rowSeparator; colSeparator; Flags } )
functions.description: Queries text of matrix for CSV export. You can use this method to quickly get all values in a given area as text. If needed, the plugin puts values in quotes and escape quotes and newlines. Please use Text.WriteTextFile to write to text file if you need CSV export.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: FirstRow
parameters.description: The index of first row. Default is 0.

parameters.name: LastRow
parameters.description: The index of last row. Default is Matrix.Height -1.

parameters.name: firstCol
parameters.description: The index of first column. Default is 0.

parameters.name: lastCol
parameters.description: The index of last column. Default is Matrix.Width -1.

parameters.name: rowSeparator
parameters.description: The row separator. Default is CR. Can be more than one character.

parameters.name: colSeparator
parameters.description: The column separator. Default is semicolon. Can be more than one character.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various flags. 1: Pass 1 to put all text values in quotes. 2: Include column names in first row (new in v13.1).

examples.label: Show matrix in dialog as CSV:

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.CSVSplit
functions.description: Splits CSV text. Plugin automatically detects if tab, comma or semicolon is used. Returns new matrix with all values. Width is defined by first row. Please call Matrix.Release later to free the matrix object.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The CSV text.

parameters.name: Delimiter
parameters.description: The delimiter to use. If empty, we auto detect it.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Pass 1 to move first row into column names.

examples.label: Test it by sparsing and putting back to CSV:
examples.text_code:
Let ([
matrix =
MBS
( "Matrix.CSVSplit"; "FirstName;LastName;City¶Joe;Miller;Las Vegas¶Bob;\"Johnson-Meyer\";\"Köln\"");
text =
MBS
("
Matrix.CSV
"; matrix);
r =
MBS
("
Matrix.Release
"; matrix)
]; text )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ([

matrix = MBS( "Matrix.CSVSplit"; "FirstName;LastName;City¶Joe;Miller;Las Vegas¶Bob;\"Johnson-Meyer\";\"Köln\"");
text = MBS("Matrix.CSV"; matrix);
r = MBS("Matrix.Release"; matrix)

]; text )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.Clear
functions.description: Clears the matrix. Removes all values from it and sets size to 0/0.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

examples.label: Clear and check:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.Clear"; $matrix) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> After this step the width and height of the matrix are 0</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Matrix Height" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.Height";$matrix) &"="& MBS("Matrix.width";$matrix) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.ConvertDataType
functions.signature: MBS( "Matrix.ConvertDataType"; MatrixRef; DataType { ; FirstRow; LastRow; firstCol; lastCol } )
functions.description: Converts the data types. We loop over all rows in all columns for the range and convert the value to the given type. If you get data from FM.SQL.Execute , we have no FM.SQL.ConvertDataType as the rows are readonly. So please convert to matrix first and use FM.SQL.ToMatrix first and then use Matrix.ConvertDataType.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: DataType
parameters.description: The new data type. Can be Number, Container, Date, Time, TimeStamp or Text.

parameters.name: FirstRow
parameters.description: The index of first row. Default is 0.

parameters.name: LastRow
parameters.description: The index of last row. Default is Matrix.Height -1.

parameters.name: firstCol
parameters.description: The index of first column. Default is 0.

parameters.name: lastCol
parameters.description: The index of last column. Default is Matrix.Width -1.

";
$matrix
; 0;"15¶07¶92" ) ]

$matrix

";
$v2

shows number as type

$v2

MBS
( "
Matrix.Release

$matrix

functions.name: Matrix.CopyContent
functions.description: Copies the content of a matrix into another matrix. Replaces the content of the second one with the content of the first one. The destination is resized if needed.

parameters.name: Matrix1
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: Matrix2
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

examples.label: Try it:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
   // new matrix
   matrix = MBS("Matrix.New"; 3; 4);
   // add some content
   r = MBS( "Matrix.SetRow"; matrix; 0; "00¶01¶02¶03" );
   r = MBS( "Matrix.SetRow"; matrix; 1; "10¶11¶12¶13" );
   r = MBS( "Matrix.SetRow"; matrix; 2; "20¶21¶22¶23" );
   // creates a second matrix
   matrix2 = MBS("Matrix.New"; 2; 2);
   // copy content of first matrix to second
   r = MBS( "Matrix.CopyContent"; matrix; matrix2  );
   // output second matrix to check
   csv = MBS("Matrix.CSV"; matrix2)
]; csv)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.Evaluate
functions.signature: MBS( "Matrix.Evaluate"; MatrixRef; Expression { ; FirstRow; LastRow; firstCol; lastCol; Flags } )
functions.description: Evaluates an expression for each cell. You can change the value by doing a calculation in FileMaker syntax to change the value. Reference current value via "current", the current row via "row" and the current column via "column" as variable names. The result of the evaluate is written to the cell. May return error if expression is invalid.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can include "row", "column" and "current" as variables.

parameters.name: FirstRow
parameters.description: The index of first row. Default is 0.

parameters.name: LastRow
parameters.description: The index of last row. Default is Matrix.Height -1.

parameters.name: firstCol
parameters.description: The index of first column. Default is 0.

parameters.name: lastCol
parameters.description: The index of last column. Default is Matrix.Width -1.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Pass 1 to ignore results of expressions and not put them back in matrix. Pass 2 to continue after error (new in 13.5).

examples.label: Try evaluate:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$matrix
; Value:
MBS
("
Matrix.New
"; 5; 6) ]
# set values
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Matrix.SetRow
";
$matrix
; 0; "Joe¶Miller¶New York¶123") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Matrix.SetRow
";
$matrix
; 1; "Bob¶Jones¶Washington¶234") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Matrix.SetRow
";
$matrix
; 2; "Jane¶Stones¶Los Angeles¶567") ]
# add one to all by expression
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Matrix.Evaluate";
$matrix
; "current + 1"; 0; 2; 3; 3) ]
# show numbers
Show Custom Dialog [ "Values" ;
MBS
("
Matrix.GetColumn
";
$matrix
; 3) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Matrix.Release
";
$matrix
) ]

examples.label: Continue on error 500 (bad date format) and remove invalid dates:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([

m = MBS("Matrix.New"; 7; 2);
s = MBS("Matrix.SetColumn"; m; 0; "10/23/71¶5.4.68¶16.555555¶garbage¶4-8-2003¶50/3/2356¶5.4.68" );
s = MBS("Matrix.SetColumn"; m; 1; "10/23/71¶5.4.68¶16.555555¶garbage¶4-8-2003¶50/3/2356¶5.4.68" );
e = "if ( GetAsText(GetAsDate ( current )) = \"?\" ; \"\" ; GetAsDate ( current ) )"; 
s = MBS("Matrix.Evaluate"; m; e; 1; 6; 1; 1; 2);

r = MBS("Matrix.Text"; m; ""; ""; ""; ""; ""; " => ")
];r)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Try to sum up a column:
examples.text_code:
# make new matrix
Set Variable [
$m
; Value:
MBS
("
Matrix.New
"; 7; 3) ]
# fill in a few values
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Matrix.SetColumn
";
$m
; 0; "1¶2¶3¶4¶5¶6¶7" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Matrix.SetColumn
";
$m
; 1; "1¶2¶3¶4¶5¶6¶7" ) ]
# now sum first and second column to go in the third column
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Matrix.Evaluate";
$m
; "
MBS
( \"
Matrix.GetValue
\";
$m
; Row; 0 )  +
MBS
( \"
Matrix.GetValue
\";
$m
; Row; 1 )"; 0;
MBS
("
Matrix.Height
";
$m
)-1; 2; 2)  ]
# output as CSV
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Matrix.CSV
";
$m
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> make new matrix</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.New"; 7; 3) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$m</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> fill in a few values</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.SetColumn"; $m; 0; "1¶2¶3¶4¶5¶6¶7" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.SetColumn"; $m; 1; "1¶2¶3¶4¶5¶6¶7" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now sum first and second column to go in the third column</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.Evaluate"; $m; "MBS( \"Matrix.GetValue\"; $m; Row; 0 )  + MBS( \"Matrix.GetValue\"; $m; Row; 1 )"; 0; MBS("Matrix.Height"; $m)-1; 2; 2)  </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> output as CSV</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Matrix.CSV"; $m) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Use column name to read value:
examples.text_code:
Let ([
// split a CSV
matrix =
MBS
( "
Matrix.CSVSplit
"; "FirstName;LastName;City¶Joe;Miller;Las Vegas¶Bob;\"Johnson-Meyer\";\"Köln\"");
// now move first row into column names
firstRow =
MBS
("
Matrix.GetRow
"; matrix; 0);
r =
MBS
("
Matrix.SetColumnNames
"; matrix; firstRow);
r =
MBS
("
Matrix.RemoveRow
"; matrix; 0);
// now you can use column names to find columns!
text =
MBS
("Matrix.GetValue"; matrix; 0; "City");
r =
MBS
("
Matrix.Release
"; matrix)
]; text )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ([
// split a CSV
matrix = MBS( "Matrix.CSVSplit"; "FirstName;LastName;City¶Joe;Miller;Las Vegas¶Bob;\"Johnson-Meyer\";\"Köln\"");
// now move first row into column names
firstRow = MBS("Matrix.GetRow"; matrix; 0);
r = MBS("Matrix.SetColumnNames"; matrix; firstRow);
r = MBS("Matrix.RemoveRow"; matrix; 0);
// now you can use column names to find columns!
text = MBS("Matrix.GetValue"; matrix; 0; "City");
r = MBS("Matrix.Release"; matrix)

]; text )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.HTML
functions.signature: MBS( "Matrix.HTML"; MatrixRef { ; FirstRow; LastRow; firstCol; lastCol; Flags; CSS Style } )
functions.description: Queries html of matrix. You can use this method to quickly get all values in a given area as html text to show in a web viewer. This joins the values and removes text formatting. Numbers, times and dates are right aligned. If the matrix has column names, we add them as headers over the html columns.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: FirstRow
parameters.description: The index of first row. Default is 0.

parameters.name: LastRow
parameters.description: The index of last row. Default is Matrix.Height -1.

parameters.name: firstCol
parameters.description: The index of first column. Default is 0.

parameters.name: lastCol
parameters.description: The index of last column. Default is Matrix.Width -1.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags for processing. Currently you can pass 1 to exclude html header and just get a HTML table.

parameters.name: CSS Style
parameters.description: Extra CSS / HTML to include.

examples.label: Show html in web viewer made from data in Excel:
examples.text_code:
# load data from Excel document
Set Variable [
$matrix
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Sheet.ReadCellsAsMatrix
";
$bookRef
;
0; // sheet index
0; // first row
9; // last row
0; // first column
5) // last column ]
# now make html
Set Variable [
$html
; Value:
MBS
( "Matrix.HTML";
$matrix
) ]
# and show in web viewer
Set Web Viewer [ Object Name: "web" ; URL: "data:text/html;charset=utf-8," &
$html
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> load data from Excel document</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Sheet.ReadCellsAsMatrix";$bookRef; 0; // sheet index0; // first row9; // last row0; // first column5) // last column </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$matrix</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now make html</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Matrix.HTML"; $matrix) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$html</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and show in web viewer</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="146" name="Set Web Viewer">
    <Action value="GoToURL"/>
    <ObjectName>
      <Calculation>"web"</Calculation>
    </ObjectName>
    <URL custom="False">
      <Calculation>"data:text/html;charset=utf-8," & $html </Calculation>
    </URL>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.IndexOfFirstValueMatching
functions.signature: MBS( "Matrix.IndexOfFirstValueMatching"; MatrixRef; Column; Operator; Value { ; StartRow } )
functions.description: Finds index of first value matching value. This uses text or numeric comparison depending on the value in the matrix field. Starting in the start row, we loop over all rows until the end and compare each value with the given operator to the value.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: Which column to search.

parameters.name: Operator
parameters.description: The compare operator. Can be =, !=, <, >, <=, >=, ≤, ≥, ≠, Prefix or Postfix.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to compare.

parameters.name: StartRow
parameters.description: The row to start at. Default is 0.

examples.label: Try it:
examples.text_code:
Let([
matrix =
MBS
("
Matrix.New
"; 5; 5);
r =
MBS
( "
Matrix.SetRow
"; matrix; 0; "1¶3" );
r =
MBS
( "
Matrix.SetRow
"; matrix; 2; "123¶5" );
r =
MBS
( "
Matrix.SetRow
"; matrix; 3; "3¶4" );
index =
MBS
( "Matrix.IndexOfFirstValueMatching"; matrix; 0; "="; 123 )
]; index)
Example result:
2

      <Calculation>Let([
matrix = MBS("Matrix.New"; 5; 5);
r = MBS( "Matrix.SetRow"; matrix; 0; "1¶3" );
r = MBS( "Matrix.SetRow"; matrix; 2; "123¶5" );
r = MBS( "Matrix.SetRow"; matrix; 3; "3¶4" );
index = MBS( "Matrix.IndexOfFirstValueMatching"; matrix; 0; "="; 123 )
]; index)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.InsertColumn

parameters.name: MatrixRef
