parameters.description: The zero based column number.

functions.name: Matrix.InsertOrUpdateRecords
functions.signature: MBS( "Matrix.InsertOrUpdateRecords"; MatrixRef; InsertFileName; InsertTableName; FieldNames; KeyFieldMapping )
functions.description: Insert or updates a lot of records queried from a database. Similar to FM.InsertOrUpdateRecordQuery , but taking records from a matrix. Copy can be from one database to other or within same database. Also from one table to other or within a table. Due to passing in new field names, you can even rearrange values from one column to other while copying. In the SQL you can use functions for sums or join data from several tables together to fill a new table. Filename parameters can be empty. Use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage and FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError to see error message. Reports an error if field name list doesn't match column count of the stored SQL result. Please remove from SQL statement and field names all fields which are globals or unstored calculations. For inserting records from other database (e.g. MySQL), please use SQL.InsertRecords function. You can specify fields and table via IDs and the plugin will lookup them for you at runtime. Table IDs and table names must be unique across all open files for this to work correctly. You can use GetFieldName() function to query field names and the plugin removes the table prefix with :: in the field name before passing it to SQL engine.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: InsertFileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the insert table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: InsertTableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into. Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: FieldNames
parameters.description: A list of field names for the insert. Empty entries in the list are ignored. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: KeyFieldMapping
parameters.description: The list of field names with index to map keys. Please specify a list of key name=key index. The key name is the name of the field in the insert table and the index gives zero-based position in result set from SELECT query in 6th parameter. Field order in source and destination does not need to be the same.

examples.label: Load records into a matrix and insert/update to other table:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$SelectSQL
; Value: "SELECT PrimaryKey, TextField, NumberField, DateField, TimeField, TimestampField, ContainerField, Description FROM Source" ]
Set Variable [
$InsertFields
; Value: "Key¶TextField¶NumberField¶DateField¶TimeField¶TimestampField¶ContainerField¶Description" ]
#
Set Variable [
$sql
; Value:
MBS
("
Matrix.NewWithSQL
"; Get(FileName);
$SelectSQL
) ]
#
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error: " &
$sql
]
Else
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("Matrix.InsertOrUpdateRecords";
$sql
; Get(FileName); "Dest";
$InsertFields
; "Key=0") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error: " &
$result
]
Else
Show Custom Dialog [ "Records updated." ;
$result
]
End If
End If
#
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Matrix.Release
";
$sql
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"SELECT PrimaryKey, TextField, NumberField, DateField, TimeField, TimestampField, ContainerField, Description FROM Source" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$SelectSQL</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Key¶TextField¶NumberField¶DateField¶TimeField¶TimestampField¶ContainerField¶Description" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$InsertFields</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.NewWithSQL"; Get(FileName); $SelectSQL) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sql</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Error: " & $sql </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.InsertOrUpdateRecords"; $sql; Get(FileName); "Dest"; $InsertFields; "Key=0") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Error: " & $result </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Records updated." </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$result </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.Release"; $sql) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.InsertOrUpdateRecordsToSQL
functions.signature: MBS( "Matrix.InsertOrUpdateRecordsToSQL"; MatrixRef; Connection; InsertTableName; FieldNames; KeyFieldMapping )
functions.description: Insert or updates a lot of records queried from a database. Reports an error if field name list doesn't match column count of the matrix.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

parameters.name: InsertTableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into. Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: FieldNames
parameters.description: A list of field names for the insert. Empty entries in the list are ignored. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: KeyFieldMapping
parameters.description: The list of field names with index to map keys. Please specify a list of key name=key index. The key name is the name of the field in the insert table and the index gives zero-based position in result set from SELECT query in 6th parameter. Field order in source and destination does not need to be the same.

examples.label: Insert or update to SQL database:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$InsertFields
; Value: "Key¶TextField¶NumberField¶DateField¶TimeField¶TimestampField¶ContainerField¶Description" ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("Matrix.InsertOrUpdateRecordsToSQL";
$sql
;
$Connection
; "Test";
$InsertFields
; "Key=0") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Key¶TextField¶NumberField¶DateField¶TimeField¶TimestampField¶ContainerField¶Description" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$InsertFields</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.InsertOrUpdateRecordsToSQL"; $sql; $Connection; "Test"; $InsertFields; "Key=0") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.InsertRecords
functions.signature: MBS( "Matrix.InsertRecords"; MatrixRef; InsertFileName; InsertTableName; FieldNames { ; StartRow; EndRow } )
functions.description: Inserts records in FileMaker database from records in memory. This function allows to easily copy a lot of records from a query in FileMaker database into a FileMaker table. Please provide field names in FileMaker. The order has to match those in the record set. Due to passing in new field names, you can even rearrange values from one column to other while copying. In the SQL you can use functions for sums or join data from several tables together to fill a new table. Use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage and FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError to see error message. Reports an error if field name list doesn't match column count of the query result. Function will fail if data types in source and dest fields do not match for assignment. e.g. date and time fields will not work, only timestamp fields. For insert from SQL Connection into FileMaker database, please use SQL.InsertRecords .

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: InsertFileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the insert table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: InsertTableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into.

parameters.name: FieldNames
parameters.description: A list of field names for the insert. Empty entries in the list are ignored.

parameters.name: StartRow
parameters.description: The index of the start row in the current recordset. First row is 0.

parameters.name: EndRow
parameters.description: The index of the end row in the current recordset. First row is 0. Last Row would be Matrix.Height -1.

functions.name: Matrix.InsertRecordsToSQL
functions.signature: MBS( "Matrix.InsertRecordsToSQL"; MatrixRef; Connection; InsertTableName; FieldNames { ; StartRow; EndRow; Replace } )
functions.description: Inserts records in SQL database from records in memory. This function allows to easily copy a lot of records from a query in FileMaker database into a SQL database. Please provide field names in other table. The order has to match those in the record set. Due to passing in new field names, you can even rearrange values from one column to other while copying. In the SQL you can use functions for sums or join data from several tables together to fill a new table. Reports an error if field name list doesn't match column count of the query result. Function will fail if data types in source and dest fields do not match for assignment. e.g. date and time fields will not work, only timestamp fields. For insert within FileMaker, please use Matrix.InsertRecords .

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

parameters.name: InsertTableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into.

parameters.name: FieldNames
parameters.description: A list of field names for the insert. Empty entries in the list are ignored.

parameters.name: StartRow
parameters.description: The index of the start row. First row is 0. Pass -1 or nothing for default which is starting with row 0.

parameters.name: EndRow
parameters.description: The index of the end row. First row is 0. Last Row would be Matrix.Height -1. Pass -1 to use all rows and the plugin will internally use RowCount-1.

parameters.name: Replace
parameters.description: Set to 1 to replace existing entries. Currently available for MySQL and databases with same syntax.

functions.name: Matrix.InsertRow
functions.description: Inserts one row into a matrix.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: Row
parameters.description: The zero based row number.

examples.label: Try it and insert one row:
examples.text_code:
Let([
matrix =
MBS
("
Matrix.New
"; 4; 4);
r =
MBS
( "
Matrix.SetRow
"; matrix; 0; "00¶01¶02¶03"  );
r =
MBS
( "
Matrix.SetRow
"; matrix; 1; "10¶11¶12¶13"  );
r =
MBS
( "
Matrix.SetRow
"; matrix; 2; "20¶21¶22¶23"  );
r =
MBS
( "
Matrix.SetRow
"; matrix; 3; "30¶31¶32¶33"  );
r =
MBS
("Matrix.InsertRow"; matrix; 2);
csv =
MBS
("
Matrix.CSV
"; matrix)
]; csv)
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
   matrix = MBS("Matrix.New"; 4; 4);
   r = MBS( "Matrix.SetRow"; matrix; 0; "00¶01¶02¶03"  );
   r = MBS( "Matrix.SetRow"; matrix; 1; "10¶11¶12¶13"  );
   r = MBS( "Matrix.SetRow"; matrix; 2; "20¶21¶22¶23"  );
   r = MBS( "Matrix.SetRow"; matrix; 3; "30¶31¶32¶33"  );
   r = MBS("Matrix.InsertRow"; matrix; 2);
   csv = MBS("Matrix.CSV"; matrix)
]; csv)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.JSONRecord
functions.description: Returns a row as JSON object. You provide list of field names, which should be in same order as fields in matrix. Optionally we can return SQL dates, times and timestamps as SQL format. Containers are returned as Base64 encoded data.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: Row
parameters.description: The row you like to get.

parameters.name: FieldNames
parameters.description: A list of field names for the JSON. If empty, we use column names, if you have some. (since 14.3)

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags for the json creation. Add 1 to get all values as text. Add 2 to get all dates, times and timestamps in SQL format. Add 4 to get arrays instead of objects for the individual rows. Add 8 to get containers as objects with name, size and data entries. (new in v12.1) Add 32 to detect JSON in text values and return the JSON inline. (New in v15.4)

examples.label: Query JSON:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$JSON
; Value:
MBS
( "Matrix.JSONRecord";
$result
; 1; "a¶b¶c"  ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Matrix.JSONRecord"; $result; 1; "a¶b¶c"  ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$JSON</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.JSONRecords
functions.signature: MBS( "Matrix.JSONRecords"; MatrixRef; FieldNames { ; Flags; FirstRow; LastRow } )
functions.description: Returns rows as JSON array. You provide list of field names, which should be in same order as fields in matrix. Optionally we can return SQL dates, times and timestamps as SQL format. Containers are returned as Base64 encoded data.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: FieldNames
parameters.description: A list of field names for the JSON. If empty, we use column names, if you have some. (since 14.3)

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags for the json creation. Add 1 to get all values as text. Add 2 to get all dates, times and timestamps in SQL format. Add 4 to get arrays instead of objects for the individual rows. Add 8 to get containers as objects with name, size and data entries. (new in v12.1) Add 16 to add field names as first row to the array if flag 4 is set. (new in v14.2) Add 32 to detect JSON in text values and return the JSON inline. (New in v15.4)

parameters.name: FirstRow
parameters.description: The index of first row. Default is 0.

parameters.name: LastRow
parameters.description: The index of last row. Default is FM.SQL.RowCount -1.

examples.label: Query matrix as JSON:
examples.text_code:
# Matrix as JSON object
Set Variable [
$JSON
; Value:
MBS
( "Matrix.JSONRecords";
$result
; "a¶b¶c"  ) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Matrix as JSON" ;
$JSON
]

  </Step>

      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.List
functions.description: Lists all IDs of current Matrix objects. Returns IDs for all Matrix objects in memory. If this list is growing, you may have forgotten to call Matrix.Release in your scripts. Normally after all scripts are done, this list should be empty.

examples.label: List all object IDs:

$list
; Value:

( "Matrix.List" ) ]

    </Value>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.Lookup
functions.signature: MBS( "Matrix.Lookup" { ; MatrixRef; Row; Column; Default Value } )
functions.description: Queries value without errors. Retains original data type. See also Matrix.GetValue function.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: Row
parameters.description: The zero based row number. Default is zero.

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The zero based column number. Default is zero.

parameters.name: Default Value
parameters.description: The default value to return in case of an error.

examples.label: Try without parameters:

("Matrix.Lookup")

functions.name: Matrix.MBS
functions.description: Calls a MBS function with parameters from the matrix. We loop over all rows and columns to collect values for parameter list. Empty cells are ignored. We pass through the result back to you.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: FunctionName
parameters.description: The name of the function to call.

examples.label: Try it:
examples.text_code:
let([
m =
MBS
("
Matrix.New
"; 3; 1);
s =
MBS
("
Matrix.SetValue
"; m; 0; 0; "Hello");
s =
MBS
("
Matrix.SetValue
"; m; 1; 0; " ");
s =
MBS
("
Matrix.SetValue
"; m; 2; 0; "World");
r =
MBS
("Matrix.
MBS
"; m; "
Text.Concat
");
m =
MBS
("
Matrix.Release
"; m)
]; r)
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>let([ 
  m = MBS("Matrix.New"; 3; 1);
  s = MBS("Matrix.SetValue"; m; 0; 0; "Hello");
  s = MBS("Matrix.SetValue"; m; 1; 0; " ");
  s = MBS("Matrix.SetValue"; m; 2; 0; "World");
  r = MBS("Matrix.MBS"; m; "Text.Concat");
  m = MBS("Matrix.Release"; m)
]; r)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.Multiply
functions.signature: MBS( "Matrix.Multiply"; MatrixRef; Value { ; FirstRow; LastRow; firstCol; lastCol } )
functions.description: Multiplies a value with cells in matrix. We loop over all rows in all columns for the range and add the given value to the numbers. If the cell doesn't contain a number, it's converted to a number like GetAsNumber().

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to use for multiplication to all cells.

parameters.name: FirstRow
parameters.description: The index of first row. Default is 0.

parameters.name: LastRow
parameters.description: The index of last row. Default is Matrix.Height -1.

parameters.name: firstCol
parameters.description: The index of first column. Default is 0.

parameters.name: lastCol
parameters.description: The index of last column. Default is Matrix.Width -1.

functions.name: Matrix.New
functions.description: Creates a new matrix (2D array). Please call Matrix.Release later to free the matrix object. Internally we store all data in FileMaker data types, so times, dates, timestamps, numbers and containers are preserved as they are and not converted to texts.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: The height of the matrix.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The width of the matrix.

functions.result: Returns matrix reference number or error.

( "
Matrix.Width
";
$matrix
) & " x " &

( "
Matrix.Height
";
$matrix
) ]

( "
Matrix.Release
";
$matrix
) ]

functions.name: Matrix.NewWithSQL
functions.description: Execute the SQL Statement against a FileMaker database. Returns a matrix reference number, so you can use functions like Matrix.Height , Matrix.Width , Matrix.GetValue and Matrix.DataType to query result. Don't forget to use Matrix.Release to release the result from memory when you are done with it. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Params parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The target database name. Can be empty to not limit query to one database.

parameters.name: SQL Statement
parameters.description: SQL Statement as a Text string

parameters.name: Params...
parameters.description: Optional, pass here parameters. One parameter to this function for each parameter you want to pass to the SQL statement.

functions.result: Returns matrix reference number or error.

functions.name: Matrix.Release
functions.description: Frees the matrix object. Releases all memory used to store the matrix itself and the values.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

$r
; Value:
MBS
("Matrix.Release";
$matrix
) ]

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.Release"; $matrix) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.SearchAndReplace
functions.signature: MBS( "Matrix.SearchAndReplace"; MatrixRef; Search Text; Replace Text { ; firstRow; LastRow; firstCol; lastCol } )
functions.description: Searches and replaces text.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: Search Text
parameters.description: The text to search.

parameters.name: Replace Text
parameters.description: The text to replace with. Can include styled text.

parameters.name: firstRow
parameters.description: The index of first row. Default is 0.

parameters.name: LastRow
parameters.description: The index of last row. Default is Matrix.Height -1.

parameters.name: firstCol
parameters.description: The index of first column. Default is 0.

parameters.name: lastCol
parameters.description: The index of last column. Default is Matrix.Width -1.

examples.label: Search and replace some text:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ([
  // split a CSV
  matrix = MBS( "Matrix.CSVSplit"; "FirstName;LastName;City¶Joe;Miller;Las Vegas¶Bob;\"Johnson-Meyer\";\"Köln\"");
  // swap first and second row
  r = MBS("Matrix.SearchAndReplace"; matrix; "ö"; "oe");
  // and ask for text back with column names in first row
  text = MBS("Matrix.CSV"; matrix);
  r = MBS("Matrix.Release"; matrix)
]; text )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

      <Calculation>$matrix) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> free</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.Release"; $matrix) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.SetColumnNames
functions.description: Sets column names. Overwrites the column names with the given list of values.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: Names
parameters.description: The value list with the names.

examples.label: Use CSV with column names:
examples.text_code:
Let ([
// split a CSV
matrix =
MBS
( "
Matrix.CSVSplit
"; "FirstName;LastName;City¶Joe;Miller;Las Vegas¶Bob;\"Johnson-Meyer\";\"Köln\"");
// now move first row into column names
firstRow =
MBS
("
Matrix.GetRow
"; matrix; 0);
r =
MBS
("Matrix.SetColumnNames"; matrix; firstRow);
r =
MBS
("
Matrix.RemoveRow
"; matrix; 0);
// and ask for text back with column names in first row
text =
MBS
("
Matrix.CSV
"; matrix;"";"";"";"";"";",";2);
r =
MBS
("
Matrix.Release
"; matrix)
]; text )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ([
// split a CSV
matrix = MBS( "Matrix.CSVSplit"; "FirstName;LastName;City¶Joe;Miller;Las Vegas¶Bob;\"Johnson-Meyer\";\"Köln\"");
// now move first row into column names
firstRow = MBS("Matrix.GetRow"; matrix; 0);
r = MBS("Matrix.SetColumnNames"; matrix; firstRow);
r = MBS("Matrix.RemoveRow"; matrix; 0);
// and ask for text back with column names in first row
text = MBS("Matrix.CSV"; matrix;"";"";"";"";"";",";2);
r = MBS("Matrix.Release"; matrix)

]; text )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.Sum
functions.description: Calculates sum value in a matrix column. You got a record set in memory and we can walk over a column in a given row range to add the sum value.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The index of the column. Default is 0.

parameters.name: FirstRow
parameters.description: The index of first row. Default is 0.

parameters.name: LastRow
parameters.description: The index of last row. Default is Matrix.Height -1.

examples.label: Sums the values in a matrix:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$matrix
; Value:
MBS
("
Matrix.New
";3;3) ]
# Set the values of the matrix
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Matrix.SetColumn
";
$matrix
; 0;"15¶07¶92" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Matrix.SetColumn
";
$matrix
; 1;"17¶3¶60" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Matrix.SetColumn
";
$matrix
; 2;"15¶1¶87" ) ]
# Add all numbers in the matrix (3x3)
Set Variable [
$a
; Value:
MBS
( "Matrix.Sum";
$matrix
; 0 ) ]
Set Variable [
$b
; Value:
MBS
( "Matrix.Sum";
$matrix
; 1 ) ]
Set Variable [
$c
; Value:
MBS
( "Matrix.Sum";
$matrix
; 2 ) ]
Set Variable [
$sum
; Value:
$a
+
$b
+
$c
]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Matrix.Release
";
$matrix
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.New";3;3) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$matrix</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Set the values of the matrix</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Matrix.SetColumn"; $matrix; 0;"15¶07¶92" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Matrix.SetColumn"; $matrix; 1;"17¶3¶60" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Matrix.SetColumn"; $matrix; 2;"15¶1¶87" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Add all numbers in the matrix (3x3)</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Matrix.Sum"; $matrix; 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$a</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Matrix.Sum"; $matrix; 1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$b</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Matrix.Sum"; $matrix; 2 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$c</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$a+$b+$c </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sum</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Matrix.Release"; $matrix) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.SwapColumns
functions.description: Swaps two columns in the matrix.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: Column1
parameters.description: The zero based column number.

parameters.name: Column2
parameters.description: The zero based column number.

examples.label: Swap columns:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ([
  // split a CSV
  matrix = MBS( "Matrix.CSVSplit"; "FirstName;LastName;City¶Joe;Miller;Las Vegas¶Bob;\"Johnson-Meyer\";\"Köln\"");
  // swap first and second row
  r = MBS("Matrix.SwapColumns"; matrix; 1; 2);
  // and ask for text back with column names in first row
  text = MBS("Matrix.CSV"; matrix);
  r = MBS("Matrix.Release"; matrix)
]; text )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Matrix.Text
functions.signature: MBS( "Matrix.Text"; MatrixRef { ; FirstRow; LastRow; firstCol; lastCol; rowSeparator; colSeparator; Flags } )
functions.description: Queries text of matrix. You can use this method to quickly get all values in a given area as text. This joins the values and removes text formatting.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: FirstRow
parameters.description: The index of first row. Default is 0.

parameters.name: LastRow
parameters.description: The index of last row. Default is Matrix.Height -1.

parameters.name: firstCol
parameters.description: The index of first column. Default is 0.

parameters.name: lastCol
parameters.description: The index of last column. Default is Matrix.Width -1.

parameters.name: rowSeparator
parameters.description: The row separator. Default is CR. Can be more than one character.

parameters.name: colSeparator
parameters.description: The column separator. Default is tab character. Can be more than one character.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various flags. 2: Include column names in first row.

functions.name: Matrix.Width
functions.description: Queries width of matrix.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

examples.label: Check size of matrix:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ MBS("Matrix.Height"; $matrix) = MBS("Matrix.Width"; $Matrix) ] 
	Show Custom Dialog [ "The matrix is square" ; "number of rows and columns are equal!" ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

$x509

$x509

$r
; Value:
MBS
( "PKCS7.Release";
$pkcs7
) ]

functions.name: SendMail.AddAttachmentContainer
functions.signature: MBS( "SendMail.AddAttachmentContainer"; EmailID; Container { ; Name; MimeType; InlineID; Method } )
functions.description: Adds an attachment to the email based on a container field. Version 5 of MBS Plugin can encode file name for preserving non ASCII characters. Still for compatibility it is recommended to use simply ASCII names. You can use Text.FileExtensionToMimeType to find the mime type for a file extension. You can use AddAttachment functions several times to add several attachments. If your Container is already Base64 encoded text, you can use the SendMail.AddAttachmentText function with 1 for IsBase64 parameter. But usually it is better to pass container directly here as Base64 has several variations with line length. For InlineID: If you like to use inline images, please check your html for references to image files. Please add all the image files as attachments with an InlineID, e.g. pass the file name as InlineID. e.g. in you html you may reference background-3.jpg in the body tag: <body background="background-3.jpg"> Add an attachment named background-3.jpg (name doesn't matter) with InlineID background-3.jpg and replace the background-3.jpg in the html with cid:background-3.jpg so the email viewer will lookup the attachment with that id to display it there.

parameters.name: EmailID
parameters.description: The email reference number obtained using SendMail.CreateEmail function.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container to attach to email.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name for the attachment. If empty, version 6.2 or newer uses file name from container.

parameters.name: MimeType
parameters.description: The mime type to use. e.g. "application/pdf" for PDF files. If missing, we use "application/octet-stream". Version 6.2 or newer detects mime type from file extension if needed.

parameters.name: InlineID
parameters.description: The content ID for inline images (CID). Empty text or no parameter gives regular attachment. You define this yourself. Can be just a file name or some uuid, which is then referenced in html with "cid:" prefix in the img src attribute.

parameters.name: Method
parameters.description: The method for the content-type parameter. Currently only for sending calendar invitiations.

examples.label: Add attachment:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SendMail.AddAttachmentContainer"; $EmailID; Attachment::Container; Attachment::Name; Attachment::Type)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

$r
; Value:
MBS
("SendMail.AddAttachmentContainer";
$EmailID
;
Attachment::ICS
; "invitation.ics"; "text/calendar"; ""; "REQUEST")]

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SendMail.AddAttachmentFile
functions.signature: MBS( "SendMail.AddAttachmentFile"; EmailID; Path { ; Name; MimeType; InlineID; Method } )
functions.description: Adds an attachment to the email based on a file path. Attachments over 100 MB are not supported. Version 5 of MBS Plugin can encode file name for preserving non ASCII characters. Still for compatibility it is recommended to use simply ASCII names. You can use Text.FileExtensionToMimeType to find the mime type for a file extension. You can use AddAttachment several times to add several attachments. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: EmailID
parameters.description: The email reference number obtained using SendMail.CreateEmail function.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the file to include as attachment.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name for the attachment. If empty, version 6.2 or newer uses file name from file path.

parameters.name: MimeType
parameters.description: The mime type to use. e.g. "application/pdf" for PDF files. If missing, we use "application/octet-stream". Version 6.2 or newer detects mime type from file extension if needed.

parameters.name: InlineID
parameters.description: The content ID for inline images (CID). Empty text or no parameter gives regular attachment. You define this yourself. Can be just a file name or some uuid, which is then referenced in html with "cid:" prefix in the img src attribute.

parameters.name: Method
parameters.description: The method for the content-type parameter. Currently only for sending calendar invitiations.

examples.label: Add attachment:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SendMail.AddAttachmentFile"; $EmailID; Attachment::Path; Attachment::Name; Attachment::Type)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Recursive custom function to add a list of files:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBSAttachment(EmailID, FilePathList, Flag_inline)

// e.g. $r=MBSattachment ( $EmailID ; MyTable::FilePathList; 1 ) ;

Case(
ValueCount (FilePathList ) > 0;
	Let(
	[
		§element = 
			ValueCount (FilePathList ) ;
		§Line = 
			GetValue( FilePathList; §element );
		§InlineID= 
			MBS( "Path.LastPathComponent"; §line ) ;
		§r= 
			MBS( 
			"SendMail.AddAttachmentFile"; 
			EmailID;
			§Line;
			""; /* file name from path automatically */
			""; /* mime name from file name automatically */
			Case(
				Flag_inline = 1;
					§InlineID ;
				""
				)
			)
	];

	// call recursive for next file:
	MBSattachment(
		EmailID; 		
		Trim(
		Substitute( 
			"¶" & FilePathList; 
			"¶" & §Line ; 
			"" 
		)); 
		Flag_inline
	)
);
	""
)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SendMail.AddAttachmentText
functions.signature: MBS( "SendMail.AddAttachmentText"; EmailID; Text; Encoding { ; Name; MimeType; InlineID; IsBase64; Method } )
functions.description: Adds an attachment to the email based on a text. Attachments over 100 MB are not supported. You can use Text.FileExtensionToMimeType to find the mime type for a file extension. You can use AddAttachment several times to add several attachments. Broken in 8.1 or older where you need to do Base64 encoding and fixed in 8.2 to work correctly.

parameters.name: EmailID
parameters.description: The email reference number obtained using SendMail.CreateEmail function.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to use for the attachment.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text parameter. Default is UTF-8. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name for the attachment. If empty, version 6.2 or newer uses file name from file path.

parameters.name: MimeType
parameters.description: The mime type to use. e.g. "application/pdf" for PDF files. If missing, we use "application/octet-stream". Version 6.2 or newer detects mime type from file extension if needed.

parameters.name: InlineID
parameters.description: The content ID for inline images (CID). Empty text or no parameter gives regular attachment.

parameters.name: IsBase64
parameters.description: Whether text is already base64 encoded. Default is 0, so we encode it. Pass 1 if you already encoded the text yourself.

parameters.name: Method
parameters.description: The method for the content-type parameter. Currently only for sending calendar invitiations.

examples.label: Adds a text attachment:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "SendMail.AddAttachmentText";
$email
; "Hello World"; "UTF-8"; "test.txt"; "text/plain" )

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SendMail.AddBCC
functions.description: Adds a blind copy recipient. See also SendMail.AddRecipient . You can call this in a loop several times to add as many recipients as needed. Please make sure the email addresses are syntactically correct.

parameters.name: EmailID

parameters.name: Email

parameters.name: Name

$EmailID
; "John@mbsplugins.test"; "John Miller")]

$EmailID
; "Bob@mbsplugins.test"; "Bob Jones")]

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SendMail.AddCC"; $EmailID; "John@mbsplugins.test"; "John Miller")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Repetition>

functions.name: SendMail.AddHeader
functions.description: Adds an header to the email. This header is passed as is and not preprocessed. You can use this function to pass custom headers for "X-Universally-Unique-Identifier:", "Date:", "X-Mailer:", "Message-Id:" and "Mime-Version:". If you pass header without text following ":", the header is not included.

parameters.name: EmailID
parameters.description: The email reference number obtained using SendMail.CreateEmail function.

parameters.name: Header
parameters.description: The new header entry. Passed to CURL later without modification. Since version 6.4 this can be a list of headers.

functions.name: SendMail.AddRecipient
functions.description: Adds a recipient. This function can be used easily if the type is also a field in your database. As an alternative you can of course also use SendMail.AddTo , SendMail.AddReplyTo , SendMail.AddCC or SendMail.AddBCC . You can call this in a loop several times to add as many recipients as needed. Please make sure the email addresses are syntactically correct.

parameters.name: EmailID
parameters.description: The email reference number obtained using SendMail.CreateEmail function.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The type of recipient. Can be TO, BCC, CC or ReplyTo.

parameters.name: Email
parameters.description: The email address of the recipient.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Optional the name of the recipient. If this name contains special characters, it will be automatically encoded with UTF-8.

examples.label: Add email address for a carbon copy:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "SendMail.AddRecipient";
$email
; "CC"; "test@mbsplugins.de"; "Bob Miller")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SendMail.AddRecipient"; $email; "CC"; "test@mbsplugins.de"; "Bob Miller")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SendMail.AddReplyTo
functions.description: Adds a recipient to reply to. If you like any receiver to email a different address for replying, you can specify this here. This allows to have bounces to to sender address while answers go to the reply-to address. See also SendMail.AddRecipient .

parameters.name: EmailID
parameters.description: The email reference number obtained using SendMail.CreateEmail function.

parameters.name: Email
parameters.description: The email address of the recipient.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Optional the name of the recipient. If this name contains special characters, it will be automatically encoded with UTF-8.

$EmailID

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SendMail.CreateEmail
functions.description: Creates a new email. The reference number returned by this function can be passed to other plugin functions for setting up the email. Later use SendMail.Release to free memory. SendMail reference numbers are starting at 14000 and counting up for each new email.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding to use for the email. If unspecified we use "UTF-8". Can be UTF-8, macintosh, windows-1250, windows-1251, windows-1252, windows-1253, windows-1254, windows-1255, windows-1256, windows-1257, ISO-8859-1, ISO-8859-2, ISO-8859-3, ISO-8859-5, ISO-8859-6, ISO-8859-7, ISO-8859-8, ISO-8859-9 or ISO-8859-15. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.name: SendMail.PrepareCURL
functions.description: Setups existing CURL session for an email transfer. You can set your own settings like proxy after this function call. If you like you can reuse the email and curl objects after you sent an email, change values and send another email. Once you passed email content to CURL, you can configure more CURL options, e.g. use CURL.SetOptionPort . When you call CURL.Perform and you get back an "OK", the email was sent to the mail server. You may see in the CURL log a message like "250 2.0.0 Ok: queued" to tell you that the email was received and stored. The email server will later decide whether your email is spam and then discarded or valid and delivered.

parameters.name: EmailID
parameters.description: The email reference number obtained using SendMail.CreateEmail function.

parameters.name: CURLID
parameters.description: The CURL session handle.

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionSSLVersion"; $curl; 6) // TLS v1.2 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SendMail.SetHTMLText
functions.description: Sets the HTML text of the new email. See also SendMail.SetPlainText and SendMail.SetRichText . If you need to encode text yourself to html, you can use the Text.EncodeToHTML function. You can read html content from file using the Text.ReplaceNewLine function. You can use Text.MergeFields to replace placeholders with values from variables, fields and expressions. Reference inline images with "cid:" prefix in the html text and add them with SendMail.AddAttachmentContainer or SendMail.AddAttachmentFile function.

parameters.name: EmailID
parameters.description: The email reference number obtained using SendMail.CreateEmail function.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The HTML text for the email. Will be encoded by the plugin to quoted printable UTF-8.

examples.label: Add inline graphics with placeholder in HTML:
examples.text_code: Set Variable [ $EmailID ; Value: MBS (" SendMail.CreateEmail ") ] # prepare HTML from field in FileMaker Set Variable [ $html ; Value: "" & GetAsCSS( EMail::PlainText ) & "" ] # Replace $$img $$ in text with image. Set Variable [ $html ; Value: Substitute( $html ; " $$img $$"; "") ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("SendMail.SetHTMLText"; $EmailID ; $html ) ] # now add a container Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" SendMail.AddAttachmentContainer "; $EmailID ; EMail::ImageContainer ; EMail::ImageName ; "image/png"; "image1.png") ]

    </Repetition>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$html</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Replace $$img$$ in text with image.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Substitute($html; "$$img$$"; "<img src=\"cid:image1.png\">") </Calculation>

    <Name>$html</Name>

functions.name: SendMail.SetSMTPServer
functions.description: Sets which SMTP server to use. There are a few combinations possible. First you can use SendMail.SetSMTPServer with SSL and pass 1 there. Then the TLS connection is right from the start. If you pass 0, you get a plain text connection, which can be upgraded using STARTTLS if you pass 3 for CURL.SetOptionUseSSL for an upgrade of the unencrypted connection to TLS later before the credentials are sent. Some servers don't support unencrypted, SSL or TLS, so only one may work. And ports can be difficult, too. You can use CURL.SetOptionPort to set a port explicit or include it in the server with double colon on the end. Common ports are 25, 587 or 465. Alternative to this method you can set CURL.SetOptionURL directly.

parameters.name: EmailID
parameters.description: The email reference number obtained using SendMail.CreateEmail function.

parameters.name: Server
parameters.description: The new SMTP Server address.

parameters.name: TLS
parameters.description: Optional, pass 1 to use TLS, else 0. Pass 0 for TLS and later configure CURL for STARTTLS. Pass 1 if you use port 465 to enable TLS.

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SendMail.CreateEmail") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$EmailID</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> ... later ...</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SendMail.SetSMTPServer"; $EmailID; "smtp.mail.me.com") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SendMail.SetSMTPUsername"; $EmailID; "someone@me.com") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SendMail.SetSMTPPassword"; $EmailID; "xxxxxx") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> ... later ...</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SendMail.PrepareCURL"; $EmailID; $curl) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> ... later ...</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionPort"; $curl; 587) // custom port </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionUseSSL"; $curl; 3) // require encryption </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionSSLVersion"; $curl; 6) // TLS v1.2 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SendMail.Sign
functions.description: Signs the email. Wraps email source with S/Mime signature. Please make sure you assemble the email before as doing changes later will remove the signature. After signing, you can use SendMail.PrepareCURL or SendMail.GetSource .

parameters.name: EmailID
parameters.description: The email reference number obtained using SendMail.CreateEmail function.

parameters.name: X509Ref
parameters.description: The reference to the X509 certificate.

parameters.name: PKeyRef
parameters.description: The reference to the private key.

examples.label: Sign an email:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$cert
; Value:
MBS
( "X509.ReadFromContainer";
EMail::Certificate
) ]
Set Variable [
$key
; Value:
MBS
( "
PKey.Read
";
EMail::PrivateKey
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("SendMail.Sign";
$EmailID
;
$cert
;
$key
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to sign email" ;
$r
]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
PKey.Release
";
$key
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "X509.Release";
$cert
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "X509.ReadFromContainer"; EMail::Certificate ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$cert</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PKey.Read"; EMail::PrivateKey ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$key</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SendMail.Sign"; $EmailID; $cert; $key) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to sign email" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PKey.Release"; $key ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "X509.Release"; $cert ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: TAPI.AddressCount
functions.description: Returns number of addresses. The local computer may have several TAPI adapters with different addresses. This function counts them.

functions.name: TAPI.AddressValue
functions.description: Queries a property of an address. Properties include: AddressName, DialableAddress, ServiceProviderName, State, DoNotDisturb and MessageWaiting. State can be InService or OutOfService.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the address. Must be in range from 0 to TAPI.AddressCount -1.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Which value to return.

functions.name: TAPI.Answer
functions.description: answers an incoming call. This function can succeed only if the call state is offering. This function checks for a license. Created 22nd October 2014 , last changed 1st December 2014 TAPI.AddressValue - TAPI.BlindTransfer 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: Call
parameters.description: The call reference number.

functions.name: TAPI.BlindTransfer
functions.description: Performs a blind or single-step transfer of the specified call to the specified destination address. The application must be the owner of the call. After a successful transfer, the call state transitions to disconnected. This function checks for a license. Created 22nd October 2014 , last changed 1st December 2014 TAPI.Answer - TAPI.CallInfo 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: Call
parameters.description: The call reference number.

parameters.name: DestAddress
parameters.description: Destination address for the transfer.

functions.name: TAPI.CallInfo
functions.description: Queries details on current call. Possible properties to query: CallerIdName: The name of the caller. CallerIdNumber: The number of the caller. CalledIdName: The name of the called location. CalledIdNumber: The number of the called location. ConnectedIdName: The name of the connected location. ConnectedIdName: The number of the connected location. RedirectionIdName: The name of the location to which a call has been redirected. RedirectionIdNumber: The number of the location to which a call has been redirected. RedirectingIdName: The name of the location that redirected the call. RedirectingIdNumber: The number of the location that redirected the call. CalledPartyFriendlyName: The called party friendly name. Comment: A comment about the call provided by the application that originated the call. DisplayableAddress: A displayable version of the called or calling address. CallingPartyID: The identifier of the calling party. State: The state of the call: Idle, InProgress, Connected, Disconnected, Offering, Hold or Queued. CountryCode: Country or region code. CallID: Call identifier. Some service providers assign a unique code to each call. Ref: Unique number of the call info object. Privilege: The privilege level. Either Owner or Monitor. Origin: Where the call is from. Can be Conference, External, InBound, Internal, OutBound, Unavailable or Unknown. Reason: Why the call was coming. Can be CallCompletion, CampedOn, Direct, ForwardedBudy, ForwardedNoAnswer, ForwardedUnconditionally, Intrude, Parked, Pickup, Redirect, Reminder, RouterRequest, Transfer, Unavailable, Unknown or Unpark. CallerIDAddressType: What type the caller ID has. CalledIDAddressType: What type the called ID has. ConnectedIDAddressType: What type the connected ID has. RedirectionAddressType: What type the redirection ID has. RedirectedAddressType: What type the redirected ID has. The address type properties on bottom define what the number properties above specify. Can be Phone Number, SDP, E-Mail Name, Domain Name or IP Address.

parameters.name: Call
parameters.description: The call reference number.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Which value to query.

functions.name: TAPI.Connect
functions.description: Attempts to complete the connection of an outgoing call.

parameters.name: Call
parameters.description: The call reference number.

parameters.name: Sync
parameters.description: Boolean indicating whether connection is to be performed synchronously (1) or asynchronously (0).

examples.label: Creates a call and connects:
examples.text_code:
$Call
=
MBS
( "
TAPI.CreateCall
"; "MyPhone"; "1234567899"; 1; 8)
$r
=
MBS
( "TAPI.Connect";
$Call
; 1 )
