    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "TAPI.CreateCall"; "MyPhone"; "1234567899"; 1; 8)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Call</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "TAPI.Connect"; $Call; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: TAPI.CreateCall
functions.description: Creates a new phone call. The new call will be the new current call, so the TAPI.Dial function (and others) should work on it. See for media types: http://msdn.microsoft.com/de-de/library/windows/desktop/ms734210(v=vs.85).aspx See for line address constants: http://msdn.microsoft.com/de-de/library/windows/desktop/ms733291(v=vs.85).aspx If your TAPI drivers are 32-bit only, it may or may not work in 64-bit.

parameters.name: AddressNameIndex
parameters.description: The index of the address or the name of the address to identify which device to use.

parameters.name: DestAddress
parameters.description: This text contains a destination address. The format is provider-specific. This can be "" for non-dialed addresses (such as with a hot phone) or when all dialing is performed using TAPI.Dial . "" in combination with a "" GroupID in TAPI.Pickpup results in a group pickup. Service providers that have inverse multiplexing capabilities can allow an application to specify multiple addresses at once.

parameters.name: AddressType
parameters.description: Contains an address type constant, such as phone number (1), which describes the format of the address. Phone Number is 1, Session Description Protocol (SDP) conference is 2, an e-mail name is 4, a domain name is 8, an IP address is 16.

parameters.name: MediaTypes
parameters.description: Identifies the media type or types that will be involved in the call session. Can be 8 for audio.

functions.result: Returns Call Reference ID or error.

examples.label: Creates a call and connects:
examples.text_code:
$Call
=
MBS
( "TAPI.CreateCall"; "MyPhone"; "1234567899"; 1; 8)
$r
=
MBS
( "
TAPI.Connect
";
$Call
; 1 )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "TAPI.CreateCall"; "MyPhone"; "1234567899"; 1; 8)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Call</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "TAPI.Connect"; $Call; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: TAPI.Dial
functions.description: Dials the specified address. You can dial on calls made with TAPI.CreateCall .

parameters.name: Call
parameters.description: The call reference number.

parameters.name: DestAddress
parameters.description: Representation of address to be dialed. The format must conform to a standard dialable address.

functions.name: TAPI.Disconnect
functions.description: Disconnects the call. The call state will transition to Disconnected after the method completes successfully. Disconnect codes: Normal: The call is being disconnected as part of the normal cycle of the call. NoAnswer: The call is being disconnected because it has not been answered. (For example, an application may set a certain amount of time for the user to answer the call. If the user does not answer, the application can call Disconnect with the NoAnswer code.) Rejected: The user rejected the offered call.

parameters.name: Call
parameters.description: The call reference number.

parameters.name: DisconnectCode
parameters.description: Can be Normal, NoAnswer or Rejected.

functions.name: TAPI.Finish
functions.description: Finishes the two-step process of transferring or adding the call to a conference.

parameters.name: Call
parameters.description: The call reference number.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: Can be "AsTransfer" or "AsConference".

functions.name: TAPI.GetCallStateEvaluate
functions.description: Queries expression to be evaluated when call changes state.

functions.name: TAPI.GetCallStateScriptFileName
functions.description: Queries the file name set for state changes on calls.

functions.name: TAPI.GetCallStateScriptName
functions.description: Queries the script name set for state changes on calls.

functions.name: TAPI.GetCallStateURL
functions.description: Queries URL to trigger for new calls.

functions.name: TAPI.GetNewCallEvaluate
functions.description: Queries expression to evaluate for new calls.

functions.name: TAPI.GetNewCallScriptFileName
functions.description: Queries the file name set for new calls.

functions.name: TAPI.GetNewCallScriptName
functions.description: Queries the script name set for new calls.

functions.name: TAPI.GetNewCallURL
functions.description: Queries URL to trigger for new calls.

functions.name: TAPI.HandoffDirect
functions.description: Hands off the call to another application based on the media type of the call. If multiple applications have registered as able to handle the types involved, TAPI will hand off to the highest-priority application, which is usually the one that registered first. This indicates that the application no longer requires ownership of the call. for media types, please take a look here: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/ms734210(v=vs.85).aspx This function checks for a license. Created 22nd October 2014 , last changed 1st December 2014 TAPI.GetNewCallURL - TAPI.Hold 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: Call
parameters.description: The call reference number.

parameters.name: MediaType
parameters.description: Media type to transfer to.

functions.name: TAPI.Hold
functions.description: Places or removes the call from the hold. This function checks for a license. Created 22nd October 2014 , last changed 1st December 2014 TAPI.HandoffDirect - TAPI.Initialize 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: Call
parameters.description: The call reference number.

parameters.name: Hold
parameters.description: Whether to put call on hold. Pass 1 for hold and 0 for unhold.

functions.name: TAPI.Initialize
functions.description: Initializes the TAPI interface. Must be called once before any other TAPI function. This loads the TAPI DLLs and initializes the drivers. Depending on your driver, it may cause some initialization code to run, which can take a while, e.g. to find the base station for the phone system in the network. If your TAPI drivers are 32-bit only, it may or may not work in 64-bit.

functions.name: TAPI.IsInitialized
functions.description: Queries TAPI initialization status. Returns 0 if not initialized or 1 if initialized.

functions.name: TAPI.ListenOnAddress
functions.description: Asks the plugin to check for one address and wait for calls. You can call this function for several addresses or just TAPI.ListenOnAllAddresses for all.

parameters.name: AddressNameIndex
parameters.description: The index of the address or the name of the address to identify which device to use.

functions.name: TAPI.ListenOnAllAddresses
functions.description: Asks the plugin to check all addresses and wait for calls.

functions.name: TAPI.ParkDirect
functions.description: Parks the call at a specified address. With directed park, the application determines the address at which it wants to park the call. With TAPI.ParkIndirect the switch determines the address and provides this to the application. In either case, a parked call can be unparked by specifying this address. The parked call enters the disconnected state after it has been successfully parked. Some switches can remind the user after a call has been parked for some long amount of time. The application sees an offering call with a call reason set to reminder.

parameters.name: Call
parameters.description: The call reference number.

parameters.name: Park
parameters.description: The address where the call is to be parked.

functions.name: TAPI.ParkIndirect
functions.description: Parks the call and returns the parked address. With TAPI.ParkDirect , the application determines the address at which it wants to park the call. With nondirected park, the switch determines the address and provides this to the application. In either case, a parked call can be unparked by specifying this address. The parked call enters the disconnected state after it has been successfully parked. Some switches can remind the user after a call has been parked for some long amount of time. The application sees an offering call with a call reason set to reminder.

parameters.name: Call
parameters.description: The call reference number.

functions.name: TAPI.Pickup
functions.description: Picks up a call alerting at the specified group identification. This function checks for a license. Created 22nd October 2014 , last changed 1st December 2014 TAPI.ParkIndirect - TAPI.Release 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: Call
parameters.description: The call reference number.

parameters.name: GroupID
parameters.description: The group identifier to which the alerting station belongs.

functions.name: TAPI.Release
functions.description: Releases one call and all it's resources used by the plugin.

parameters.name: Call
parameters.description: The call reference number.

functions.name: TAPI.ReleaseAll
functions.description: Releases all calls known by the plugin. Be aware that on a server this may free objects, which are used by other scripts running in parallel!

examples.label: Cleanup:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "TAPI.ReleaseAll" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "TAPI.ReleaseAll" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: TAPI.RemoveFromConference
functions.description: Removes the call from a conference if it is involved in one. This function checks for a license. Created 22nd October 2014 , last changed 1st December 2014 TAPI.ReleaseAll - TAPI.SetCallStateEvaluate 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: Call
parameters.description: The call reference number.

functions.name: TAPI.SetCallStateEvaluate
functions.description: Sets the expression to evaluate when a call changes state. The $$ID$$ in evaluate is replaced with the reference number of the call as defined by our plugin. You can then use TAPI.CallInfo and other functions to query information.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

functions.name: TAPI.SetCallStateScript
functions.description: Sets which script to call when a call changes state. The script is called when an existing call changes state. The parameter to your script will be the reference number of the call as defined by our plugin. You can then use TAPI.CallInfo and other functions to query information like the state of the call. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

functions.name: TAPI.SetCallStateURL
functions.description: Sets the URL to query when call changes state. $$ID$$ in the URL is replaced with call ID. Queries http/ftp/sftp URLs with CURL, other URLs passed to OS to open.

parameters.name: URL
parameters.description: The URL to trigger.

functions.name: TAPI.SetNewCallEvaluate
functions.description: Sets the expression to evaluate when a new call arrives. The $$ID$$ in evaluate is replaced with the reference number of the call as defined by our plugin. You can then use TAPI.CallInfo and other functions to query information.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

functions.name: TAPI.SetNewCallScript
functions.description: Sets which script to call when a new call is detected. The parameter to your script will be the reference number of the call as defined by our plugin. You can then use TAPI.CallInfo and other functions to query information. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

examples.label: Set the script to trigger for new calls:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("TAPI.SetNewCallScript"; Get(FileName); "NewCallScript") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("TAPI.SetNewCallScript"; Get(FileName); "NewCallScript") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: TAPI.SetNewCallURL
functions.description: Sets the URL to query when new call arrives. $$ID$$ in the URL is replaced with menu ID. Queries http/ftp/sftp URLs with CURL, other URLs passed to OS to open.

parameters.name: URL
parameters.description: The URL to trigger.

functions.name: TAPI.Shutdown
functions.description: Shuts down TAPI support. This function frees all system resources allocated for TAPI. This function checks for a license. Created 22nd October 2014 , last changed 22nd October 2014 TAPI.SetNewCallURL - TAPI.Transfer 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: TAPI.Transfer
functions.description: Transfers the current call to the destination address. Call transfer involves setting up a consultation call in preparation for the transfer. Call2 is the identifier returned TAPI.CreateCall following the creation of a consultation call. TAPI.Finish (with AsTransfer) completes the transfer. If the consultation call is not in the Connected state when Transfer is called, TAPI will use the destination address (as specified when the consultation call was first created via TAPI.CreateCall and try to connect at that time. If the original call had an empty destination address, Transfer will fail with error (invalid argument).

parameters.name: Call
parameters.description: The call reference number.

parameters.name: OtherCall
parameters.description: The other call reference number. This is the consultation call created for the transfer.

parameters.name: Sync
parameters.description: Indicates whether the method should be completed synchronously (1) or asynchronously (0).

functions.name: TAPI.Unpark
functions.description: Gets the call from park. This function checks for a license. Created 22nd October 2014 , last changed 1st December 2014 TAPI.Transfer - TOTP.Calculate 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: Call
parameters.description: The call reference number.

functions.name: Twain.Acquire
functions.description: Acquires a picture. The acquire process will go asynchronously. You can define a script with Twain.SetNotificationScript to be called when transfers are done. If you acquire without UI, you can make script pause to let the acquire work and check with Twain.GetImageCount function whether images have been received. Even when using no UI, you can pass Modal = 1 to have the plugin transfer the image right after acquire without waiting for an event from twain driver.

parameters.name: Modal
parameters.description: Whether to run the process modal. Default is 0 for running non modal. Ignored in most cases.

parameters.name: ShowUI
parameters.description: Whether to show the dialog. Pass 1 to show dialog or 0 to not show it. Default is 1.

parameters.name: BringWindowToFront
parameters.description: For Windows only a flag. Pass 1 to have the plugin watch for new windows and bring them to front and avoid them showing behind FileMaker’s windows. Default 0.

functions.result: Returns OK or the Twain error code.

examples.label: Acquire with UI, window to front and modal:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Twain.Acquire"; 1; 1; 1)
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Twain.Acquire"; 1; 1; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Twain.ClearCancelScript
functions.description: Clears the cancel script. See also Twain.SetCancelScript .

functions.name: Twain.ClearNotificationScript
functions.description: Clears the notification script. See also Twain.SetNotificationScript .

functions.name: Twain.CloseDS
functions.description: Closes data source. This function is called automatically if needed. Call it only if you know what you do.
functions.result: Returns OK or the Twain error code.

functions.name: Twain.CloseDSM
functions.description: Closes data source manager. This function is called automatically if needed. Call it only if you know what you do.

functions.name: Twain.CurrentDSIdentity
functions.description: Queries current data source. Possible selector values: Manufacturer, ProductFamily, ProductName, SupportedGroups, ProtocolMajor, Id, VersionMajorNum, VersionMinorNum, VersionLanguage, VersionCountry or VersionInfo.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Optional, which value to query. If you have no selector string, you receive the product name.

functions.result: Returns requested value or error message.

functions.name: Twain.CurrentImage
functions.description: Queries the current image from last query. Returns the image reference for use with GMImage functions. Please use functions like GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer and later GMImage.Release to free memory. If you get no image here, you may have called Twain.SetFolderPath before and images are written to folder.

functions.name: Twain.DisableDS
functions.description: Disables the data source. This function is called automatically if needed. Call it only if you know what you do. This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 Twain.CurrentImage - Twain.GetAutofeed 💬 Ask a question or report a problem
functions.result: Returns OK or the Twain error code.

functions.name: Twain.GetAutofeed
functions.description: Queries whether plugin should auto feed (if feeder is enabled). Value is -1 if you didn't yet call Twain.SetAutofeed before.

functions.name: Twain.GetAutomaticBorderDetection
functions.description: Queries whether plugin should enable automatic border detection. Value is -1 if you didn't yet call Twain.SetAutomaticBorderDetection before. 0 = off, 1 = on, -1 = default/undefined.

functions.name: Twain.GetAutomaticBrightness
functions.description: Queries the Source's Auto-brightness function (if any). The setting is -1 if not set.

functions.name: Twain.GetAutomaticRotate
functions.description: Queries whether plugin should enable automatic rotation. Value is -1 if you didn't yet call Twain.SetAutomaticRotate before.

functions.name: Twain.GetBrightness
functions.description: Queries the brightness setting to use. Source should normalize the values into the range. Make sure that a '0' value is available as the Current Value when the Source starts up. If the Source's ± range is asymmetric about the '0' value, set range maxima to ±1000 and scale homogeneously from the '0' value in each direction. This will yield a positive range whose step size differs from the negative range's step size. Plugin uses value -10000 for undefined/default.

functions.name: Twain.GetCloseAfterTransfer
functions.description: Whether to close window and data source after a transfer. GUI is only closed when being used.

functions.name: Twain.GetCompressionQuality
functions.description: Queries the current quality setting. Use Twain.SetCompressionQuality to set this.

functions.name: Twain.GetContrast
functions.description: Queries which contrast value to use. Scale the values available internally into a homogeneous range between -1000 and 1000. Make sure that a '0' value is available as the Current value when the Source starts up. If the Source's ± range is asymmetric about the '0' value, set range maxima to ±1000 and scale homogeneously from the '0' value in each direction. This will yield a positive range whose step size differs from the negative range's step size. Plugin uses value -10000 for undefined/default.

functions.name: Twain.GetDSIdentitiesCount
functions.description: Queries number of data sources.

functions.name: Twain.GetDSIdentity
functions.description: Queries data source with given index. Possible selector values: Manufacturer, ProductFamily, ProductName, SupportedGroups, ProtocolMajor, Id, VersionMajorNum, VersionMinorNum, VersionLanguage, VersionCountry or VersionInfo.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the data source. From 1 to Twain.GetDSIdentitiesCount .

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Optional, which value to query. If you have no selector string, you receive the product name.

functions.result: Returns requested value or error message.

functions.name: Twain.GetDefaultIdentity
functions.description: Queries default data source. Possible selector values: Manufacturer, ProductFamily, ProductName, SupportedGroups, ProtocolMajor, Id, VersionMajorNum, VersionMinorNum, VersionLanguage, VersionCountry or VersionInfo.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Optional, which value to query. If you have no selector string, you receive the product name.

functions.result: Returns requested value or error message.

functions.name: Twain.GetDiscardBlankPages
functions.description: Queries whether plugin should enable feeder. Value is -1 if you didn't yet call Twain.SetDiscardBlankPages before.

functions.name: Twain.GetDuplexEnabled
functions.description: Queries whether plugin should enable feeder. Value is -1 if you didn't yet call Twain.SetDuplexEnabled before.

functions.name: Twain.GetFeededEnabled
functions.description: Queries whether plugin should enable feeder. Value is -1 if you didn't yet call Twain.SetFeededEnabled before.

functions.name: Twain.GetFilePaths
functions.description: Queries the list of file paths used to create files. This is set by Twain.Acquire .

functions.name: Twain.GetFileType
functions.description: Queries the current file type.

functions.name: Twain.GetFolderPath
functions.description: Queries the folder path. Set by Twain.SetFolderPath .

functions.name: Twain.GetGamma
functions.description: Queries the gamma correction value for the image data. Default value 2.2. The setting is -10000 if not set.

functions.name: Twain.GetHighlight
functions.description: Queries which value to use for highlight. Value can be between 0 and 255. Plugin uses value -10000 for undefined/default.

functions.name: Twain.GetImageCount
functions.description: Queries the number of images in memory. A scan may give several images. So the plugin caches them and you can query them in a loop with Twain.CurrentImage .
functions.result: Returns number of images in memory.

functions.name: Twain.GetOrientation
functions.description: Queries current orientation.

functions.name: Twain.GetPaper
functions.description: Queries the paper to use. This queries the setting you made with Twain.SetPaper .

functions.name: Twain.GetPixelType
functions.description: Queries current pixel type setting. Value is -1 if not yet set.

functions.name: Twain.GetResolutionX
functions.description: Queries the current resolution setting for the next scan. Value is -1 if you didn't call Twain.SetResolutionX before.

functions.name: Twain.GetResolutionY
functions.description: Queries the current resolution setting for the next scan. Value is -1 if you didn't call Twain.SetResolutionY before.

functions.name: Twain.GetShadow
functions.description: Queries which value in an image should be interpreted as the darkest shadow. All values darker then this value will be clipped to this value. Whether darker values are smaller or larger can be determined by examining the Current value of PixelFlavor. Source If more or less than 8 bits are used to describe the image, the actual data values should be normalized to fit within the 0-255 range. The normalization need not result in a homogeneous distribution if the original distribution was not homogeneous. Plugin uses value -10000 for undefined/default.

functions.name: Twain.GetUIVisible
functions.description: Queries internal state from plugin about whether a TWAIN dialog is visible currently.

functions.name: Twain.ImageInfo
functions.description: Queries image details for last scanned image. Possible keys:

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Which information you want to have.

functions.name: Twain.IsDSEnabled
functions.description: Whether data source is enabled. This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 Twain.Initialize - Twain.IsInitialized 💬 Ask a question or report a problem
functions.result: Returns 1 if enabled and 0 if not.

functions.name: Twain.IsInitialized
functions.description: Checks if Twain library has been initialized. If value is 1, everything is okay. If value is zero, the library is not initialized and you need to call Twain.Initialize function. On a hosted database this function is better than using a global variable, as the variable can't see if initialization was made on server or client side.

functions.name: Twain.OpenDS
functions.description: Opens a data source. This function is called automatically if needed. Call it only if you know what you do.
functions.result: Returns OK or the Twain error code.

functions.name: Twain.OpenDSM
functions.description: Opens data source manager. This function is called automatically if needed. Call it only if you know what you do.

functions.name: Twain.SelectDS
functions.description: Queries dialog to ask the user to select a data source.
functions.result: Returns OK or the Twain error code.

functions.name: Twain.SelectDSIdentity
functions.description: Sets current data source.

parameters.name: IndexOrName
parameters.description: Which data source to use. Can be name of product or index in list of identities.

functions.name: Twain.SetAutofeed
functions.description: Whether to enable auto feed when next scan starts. This value is stored and next time you call Acquire the plugin will ask the scanner to enable/disable auto feed. If the scanner does not support auto feeding, the scanner will ignore this setting.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new settings. Can be 1 to enable auto feeder or 0 to disable.

examples.label: Set options and acquire:
examples.text_code:
$r
=
MBS
( "
Twain.SetCloseAfterTransfer
"; 1 )
$r
=
MBS
( "
Twain.SetFeededEnabled
"; 1 )
$r
=
MBS
( "Twain.SetAutofeed"; 1 )
$r
=
MBS
( "
Twain.Acquire
" )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

functions.name: Twain.SetAutomaticBorderDetection
functions.description: Whether to enable automatic border detection when next scan starts. This value is stored and next time you call Acquire the plugin will ask the scanner to enable/disable automatic border detection. If the scanner does not support automatic border detection, the scanner will ignore this setting and set it back to -1. 0 = off, 1 = on, -1 = default/undefined.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new settings. Can be 1 to enable automatic border detection or 0 to disable.

functions.name: Twain.SetAutomaticBrightness
functions.description: Enables or disables the Source's Auto-brightness function (if any). The setting is -1 if not set.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new settings. Can be 1 to enable automatic brightness or 0 to disable.

functions.name: Twain.SetAutomaticRotate
functions.description: Whether to enable automatic rotation when next scan starts. This value is stored and next time you call Acquire the plugin will ask the scanner to enable/disable automatic rotation. If the scanner does not support automatic rotation, the scanner will ignore this setting and set it back to -1.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new settings. Can be 1 to enable automatic rotation or 0 to disable.

functions.name: Twain.SetBrightness
functions.description: Sets the brightness setting to use. Source should normalize the values into the range. Make sure that a '0' value is available as the Current Value when the Source starts up. If the Source's ± range is asymmetric about the '0' value, set range maxima to ±1000 and scale homogeneously from the '0' value in each direction. This will yield a positive range whose step size differs from the negative range's step size. Plugin uses value -10000 for undefined/default.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new brightness setting.

functions.name: Twain.SetCancelScript
functions.description: Sets the script to call when a dialog was shown and the user pressed Cancel button. This may also be called on errors. The plugin simply calls whenever the dialog closes and we have no images. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.description: The parameter to pass.

examples.label: Registers fail handler:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Twain.SetCancelScript"; Get ( FileName ); "CancelledScript")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Twain.SetCancelScript"; Get ( FileName ); "CancelledScript")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Twain.SetContrast

Make sure that a '0' value is available as the Current value when the Source starts up. If the Source's ± range is asymmetric about the '0' value, set range maxima to ±1000 and scale homogeneously from the '0' value in each direction. This will yield a positive range whose step size differs from the negative range's step size. Plugin uses value -10000 for undefined/default.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new brightness setting.

parameters.name: Value

functions.name: Twain.SetFileType
functions.description: Sets the file type to use for writing image files. Only used if you use Twain.SetFolderPath . For PDF, you can use DynaPDF / PDFKit functions to convert image to PDF.

parameters.name: FileType
parameters.description: The new file type. Can be BMP, JPEG, TIFF, PNG or GIF. Default is JPEG.

functions.name: Twain.SetFolderPath
functions.description: Sets the folder path for storing image files. If you set the path with this function, images are not available with the Twain.CurrentImage function and Twain.ImageCount returns always zero. But with Twain.GetFilePaths you can learn where files have been stored. The path must end with a slash on Mac and a backslash on Windows. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path to the folder to use for storing image files. If empty clears the file path

functions.name: Twain.SetGamma
functions.description: Sets the gamma correction value for the image data. Default value 2.2. The setting is -10000 if not set.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new brightness setting.

functions.name: Twain.SetHighlight
functions.description: Specifies which value in an image should be interpreted as the lightest highlight. All values lighter then this value will be clipped to this value. Whether lighter values are smaller or larger can be determined by examining the Current value of PixelFlavor. If more or less than 8 bits are used to describe the image, the actual data values should be normalized to fit within the 0-255 range. The normalization need not result in a homogeneous distribution if the original distribution was not homogeneous. Value can be between 0 and 255. Plugin uses value -10000 for undefined/default.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new brightness setting.

functions.name: Twain.SetNotificationScript
functions.description: Sets the script to call when a transfer is complete and you can query image. In the target script you can call ImageInfo and CurrentImage functions to query image and process it. For example save it as JPEG or PNG in a container. Of course the JPEG or PNG can easily be added to a PDF file. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.description: The parameter to pass.

examples.label: Registers fail handler:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Twain.SetNotificationScript"; Get ( FileName ); "GotImage")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Twain.SetNotificationScript"; Get ( FileName ); "GotImage")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Twain.SetOrientation
functions.description: Sets current orientation. This value is stored and next time you call Acquire the plugin will ask the scanner to enable/disable feeder. If the scanner does not support the feeder, the scanner will ignore this setting. Value can be:

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: the new orientation value.

functions.name: Twain.SetPaper
functions.description: Requests to use a certain paper. This is what the plugin will ask the scanner to provide. If scanner doesn't support it (or driver), we ignore the request. Possible paper values:

parameters.name: Paper
parameters.description: The paper ID to request.

functions.name: Twain.SetPaperSize
functions.description: Requests a page size in inches. If scanner doesn't support this, we ignore the request.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: Width of paper in inches.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: Height of paper in inches.

functions.name: Twain.SetPixelType
functions.description: Sets the pixel type requested on next Twain.Acquire command. Value can be 0 for BW, 1 for Gray, 2 for RGB, 3 for Palette. The plugin can request als CMY (4), CMYK (5), YUV (6), YUVK (7) and CIEXYZ (8), but can't currently decode those.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new pixel type setting.

functions.name: Twain.SetResolutionX
functions.description: Sets the horizontal resolution for scanning. This value is stored and next time you call Acquire the plugin will ask the scanner to use this resolution. If the scanner does not support the resolution, the scanner will use the last valid setting. If the scanner only supports certain resolutions as with HP M175 (75, 100, 200, 300, 600, 1200), use only one of the resolutions according the GUI Acquire settings in the dialogue. In this case "150" is not possible!

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The horizontal resolution value.

functions.name: Twain.SetResolutionY
functions.description: Sets the vertical resolution for scanning. This value is stored and next time you call Acquire the plugin will ask the scanner to use this resolution. If the scanner does not support the resolution, the scanner will use the last valid setting. If the scanner only supports certain resolutions as with HP M175 (75, 100, 200, 300, 600, 1200), use only one of the resolutions according the GUI Acquire settings in the dialogue. In this case "150" is not possible!

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The vertical resolution value.

functions.name: Twain.SetShadow
functions.description: Specifies which value in an image should be interpreted as the darkest shadow. All values darker then this value will be clipped to this value. Whether darker values are smaller or larger can be determined by examining the Current value of PixelFlavor. Source If more or less than 8 bits are used to describe the image, the actual data values should be normalized to fit within the 0-255 range. The normalization need not result in a homogeneous distribution if the original distribution was not homogeneous. Plugin uses value -10000 for undefined/default.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new brightness setting.

functions.name: Twain.SupportsBW
functions.description: Whether device supports black and white pixel type.
functions.result: Returns 1 if RGB is supported and 0 if not. Or error.

functions.name: Twain.SupportsGray
functions.description: Whether current device supports gray pixel type.
functions.result: Returns 1 if RGB is supported and 0 if not. Or error.

functions.name: Twain.SupportsMemoryTransfer
functions.description: Whether device supports memory transfers. This function is free to use. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 Twain.SupportsGray - Twain.SupportsRGB 💬 Ask a question or report a problem
functions.result: Returns 1 if memory transfers are supported and 0 if not. Or error.

functions.name: Twain.SupportsRGB
functions.description: Whether current device supports RGB pixel type.
functions.result: Returns 1 if RGB is supported and 0 if not. Or error.

functions.name: Twain.TransferImage
functions.description: Transfers an image. This function is called automatically if needed. Call it only if you know what you do. This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 Twain.SupportsRGB - UNNotification.Action 💬 Ask a question or report a problem
functions.result: Returns OK or the Twain error code.

functions.name: Variable.Add
functions.description: Adds a value to a given variable atomically. Internally does similar to Variable.Get to read value, then adds given parameter and stores like Variable.Set , all in one operation, so no other script or calculation can get between. Creates variable, if it's not existing yet.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: the name of the variable

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: the value to add to the variable

examples.label: Sets $ID and counts up with each call:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$ID
; Value:
MBS
( "Variable.Add"; "ID"; 1 ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Variable.Add"; "ID"; 1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ID</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Add values:
examples.text_code:
Let([
c =
MBS
( "
Variable.Clear
"; "ID" );
v1 =
MBS
( "Variable.Add"; "ID"; 1 );
v2 =
MBS
( "Variable.Add"; "ID"; 1 );
v3 =
MBS
( "Variable.Add"; "ID"; 1 );
v4 =
MBS
( "
Variable.Get
"; "ID")
]; v1 & v2 & v3 & " " & v4)
Example result:
123 3
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
 c = MBS( "Variable.Clear"; "ID" );
 v1 = MBS( "Variable.Add"; "ID"; 1 );
 v2 = MBS( "Variable.Add"; "ID"; 1 );
 v3 = MBS( "Variable.Add"; "ID"; 1 );
 v4 = MBS( "Variable.Get"; "ID")
]; v1 & v2 & v3 & " " & v4)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Try in a script:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Variable.Clear
"; "Total")]
Set Variable [
$r1
; Value:
MBS
("Variable.Add"; "Total"; 10)]
Set Variable [
$r2
; Value:
MBS
("Variable.Add"; "Total"; 25)]
Set Variable [
$sum
; Value:
MBS
("
Variable.Get
"; "Total")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Sum Total";
$sum
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Variable.Clear"; "Total")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Variable.Add"; "Total"; 10)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Variable.Add"; "Total"; 25)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Variable.Get"; "Total")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sum</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Sum Total"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$sum</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Try the function:
examples.text_code:
# Step 1: Clear any previous variable
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Variable.Clear
"; "PeopleList")]
# Step 2: Add values to the variable list
Set Variable [
$r1
; Value:
MBS
("Variable.AppendValue"; "PeopleList"; "Alice")]
Set Variable [
$r2
; Value:
MBS
("Variable.AppendValue"; "PeopleList"; "Bob")]
Set Variable [
$r3
; Value:

("Variable.AppendValue"; "PeopleList"; "Charlie")]
# Step 3: Retrieve the value as a return-delimited list

$peopleList
; Value:
MBS
("
Variable.Get
"; "PeopleList")]

$peopleList
]

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>

functions.name: Variable.Get
functions.description: Retrieves the value of named variable that was declared using Variable.Set . Unlike the variables that were introduced in FileMaker 8 these variables are not limited to the File or Script. In other words they can be retrieved from any file in the system regardless of where they were declared. You can check with Variable.Exists whether a variable exists. Returns an error if variable doesn't exist. Use Variable.Lookup to lookup without getting an error if the variable doesn't exist.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The Name of the variable

examples.label: Retrieve a variable

    </Value>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Variable.Lookup

Unlike the variables that were introduced in FileMaker 8 these variables are not limited to the File or Script. In other words they can be retrieved from any file in the system regardless of where they were declared. You can check with Variable.Exists whether a variable exists.

parameters.description: The Name of the variable

parameters.name: DefaultValue

functions.name: Variable.Set
functions.description: Stores a named variable that can be retrieved later using Variable.Get . Unlike the variables that were introduced in FileMaker 8 these variables are not limited to the File or Script. In other words they can be retrieved from any file in the system regardless of where they were declared. All values are stored in their native data types (up to version 4.4 only text). Use Variable.Clear to clear the variables or Variable.ClearAll to clear them all. Variable.List returns list of all defined variables. On server, variables are shared by all users connected to the same server and will persist between sessions. So, for example, all Web Direct users will have the same set of global variables set via the plugin. You can optionally prefix variables with a user ID/Name to make them per-user unique. Variables will stay in memory until explicitly released; or the process quits. If you'd like to share data between multiple processes (e.g. Web Direct, Data API and Server Scripting), please use the SharedMemory functions like SharedMemory.SetValue .

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: the name of the variable

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: the value to assign to the variable

examples.label: Set a variable
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Variable.Set" ; "myVar"; "Hello" )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Variable.Set" ; "myVar"; "Hello" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebHook.Check
functions.description: Checks whether a web hook has been triggered. Returns list of web request reference numbers for new requests received. Please check them with WebRequest functions and then call later WebRequest.Release to free object. This lets you open a network port for incoming HTTP Requests, e.g. from a web service like your phone system.

parameters.name: WebHook
parameters.description: The reference number of the web hook. Pass a web hook reference if you like to check only for that web hook. If parameter is empty or missing, we return all pendign web requests.

examples.label: Looping script to process incoming requests:
examples.text_code:
Loop
# check for pending requests
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:
MBS
( "WebHook.Check" ) ]
#
# loop over pending requests
Set Variable [
$count
; Value: ValueCount(
$list
) ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 1 ]
If [
$index
≤
$count
]
Loop
Set Variable [
$ref
; Value: GetValue(
$list
;
$index
) ]
Perform Script [ Specified: From list ; “WebHookReceived on server” ; Parameter:
$ref
]
#
# next
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
>
$count
]
End Loop
End If
#
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
End Loop
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> check for pending requests</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebHook.Check" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> loop over pending requests</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>ValueCount($list) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $index ≤ $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue($list; $index) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ref</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="1" name="Perform Script">
    <Calculation>$ref</Calculation>
    <Script id="99999" name="WebHookReceived on server"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index > $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebHook.Create
functions.description: Creates a new web hook object. Please call WebHook.Release later to free it. You can decide whether you want your web hook always answer the same answer with auto answering (defaults to HTTP 200 OK as response) or whether you like to disable that, keep connection open and answer the query in a timely manner in the scripts. But if FileMaker is busy, you may be too late (2 minutes usually) and the connection is already timed out.

examples.label: Start a normal HTTP Webhook:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$$WebHooks
; Value:
MBS
("WebHook.Create") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
WebHook.Listen
";
$$WebHooks
; 9999) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
WebHook.SetScript
";
$$WebHooks
; Get(FileName); "WebHookReceived") ]

    </Repetition>

	Set Field [ WebHook::Server Webhook ; $$WebHooks ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebHook.SetAutoAnswer
functions.description: Sets auto answer text. This is the http response we send out for a request. We send out a text like this by default: HTTP/1.1 200 OK Server: MBS Plugin Connection: close Content-Length: 0 Line endings must be CRLF, so please use Text.ReplaceNewLine function if needed. Can be set to empty text if you plan to use WebHook.SetMode and WebRequest.Send functions.

parameters.description: The reference number of the web hook.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to send out. Should use CRLF as line endings and end with two CRLF.

parameters.name: Encoding

functions.name: WebHook.SetMaximumRequestSize
functions.description: Sets maximum allowed request size. This allows you to disconnect sockets, which send too much data. By default there is no limit, so people can send requests with giga byte big payload. Depending on your use can it may be worth to limit request size to a reasonable value. For example if you receive notifications from your phone system, you may set the maximum size to 10000 bytes and a timeout of 60 seconds for WebHook.SetTimeOut function. That is probably 10 times more than needed, but may avoid some malicious user from sending a ton of useless traffic. The check is performed after we check HTTP requests, so if a request comes quickly, it may skip the limit check. If you test this on localhost, you need to e.g. use --limit-rate with curl command line tool to slow down your request. This is not an issue over a regular network with latency.

parameters.name: WebHook
parameters.description: The reference number of the web hook.

parameters.name: MaxBytes
parameters.description: The allowed maximum value. Can be 0 for no limit.

functions.name: WebHook.SetMode
functions.description: Sets mode flags. Pass 1 for keeping connection open. Pass 2 for accepting non HTTP connections, e.g. to accept notifications from devices like scales. If connection stays open, you can send a custom answer in the triggered script via WebRequest.Send function. Pass also empty text for WebHook.SetAutoAnswer to disable auto answering. The RAW connection option allows you to accept other data than HTTP requests. Use WebRequest.GetRawData to read the data coming in. Use either WebHook.SetAutoAnswer to automatically send data or use flag 1 to also keep connection open to use WebRequest.Send function to send your answer.

parameters.name: WebHook
parameters.description: The reference number of the web hook.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: The new mode.

functions.name: WebHook.SetSSL
functions.description: Sets SSL certificate and key to use. We can then accept connections with TLS v1.2 or newer. You can create your own SSL key and self signed certificate with openssl in the terminal: openssl genpkey -out private_key.pem -algorithm RSA -pkeyopt rsa_keygen_bits:2048 Or with password: openssl genpkey -aes-256-cbc -algorithm RSA -out private_key.pem -pkeyopt rsa_keygen_bits:4096 openssl req -new -key private_key.pem -out device1.csr openssl x509 -req -days 365 -in device1.csr -signkey private_key.pem -out certificate.crt Alternatively you can use the web server as proxy to do SSL and forward the call it gets at e.g. https://domainname/webhook to http://domainname:xxxx internally with some random port number you picked. This way the web hook is not exposed directly to the outside, but stays behind the web server and the firewall.

parameters.name: WebHook
parameters.description: The reference number of the web hook.

parameters.name: CertificateFile
parameters.description: The native file path to the certificate file. The file may include more than one certificate if you have an intermediary one.

parameters.name: PrivateKeyFile
parameters.description: The native file path to the private key file.

parameters.name: Password
parameters.description: The password needed to decrypt private key.

$$WebHooks

$r

MBS
("
WebHook.Listen
";
$$WebHooks
; 9999) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
WebHook.SetScript
";
$$WebHooks
; Get(FileName); "WebHookReceived") ]

functions.name: WebHook.SetScript
functions.description: Sets the script to call when request was received. For server, please use WebHook.Check in a looping script to check regularly for new requests. If you use AutoAnswer and you don't keep connection open, you can check for new requests once a minute with a scheduled script. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: WebHook
parameters.description: The reference number of the web hook.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name for the script to trigger.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name of the script to launch.

("WebHook.SetScript";
$$WebHooks
; Get(FileName); "WebHookReceived") ]

functions.name: WebHook.SetTag
functions.description: Sets the tag value. The tag value allows you to store any value related to the web hook here like a RecordID in FileMaker.

parameters.name: WebHook
parameters.description: The reference number of the web hook.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new tag value.

functions.name: WebHook.SetTimeOut
functions.description: Sets the timeout for the hook. Default is to close socket 5 minutes after last data was sent/received to avoid open connections linger around forever. For example if you receive notifications from your phone system, you may set the maximum size to 10000 bytes (via WebHook.SetMaximumRequestSize function) and a timeout of 60 seconds. That is probably 10 times more than needed, but may avoid some malicious user from sending a ton of useless traffic.

parameters.name: WebHook
parameters.description: The reference number of the web hook.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The timeout value in seconds. Default is 300 for 5 minutes.

functions.name: WordFile.Append
functions.description: Appends one word file text to other. This function takes document part from other word file and copies it’s xml nodes to the current document. Please only copy from one document to other if they both are made from same template, so internal IDs for styles match.

parameters.name: WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

parameters.name: Other WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the second word file.

parameters.name: AddPageBreak
parameters.description: Whether to add a page break.

functions.name: WordFile.AppendTableRow
functions.description: Duplicates and appends a table row containing a placeholder. If you have tables in your template word file and you like to duplicate rows and add them to the end of the table, just call this function with a placeholder field in that row. The place holder in the document must start with « and end with » characters. Use WordFile.AppendTableRow to add new row to the end or WordFile.DuplicateTableRow to add it after the referenced one.

parameters.name: WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

parameters.name: Placeholder
parameters.description: The name of the placeholder.

functions.name: WordFile.Contains
functions.description: Checks if some text exists in the document. This is like WordFile.HasTag function, but doesn't require markers around the text in the document.

parameters.name: WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

parameters.name: FindText
parameters.description: The text to look for.

functions.name: WordFile.DuplicateTableRow
functions.description: Duplicates a table row containing a placeholder. If you have tables in your template word file and you like to duplicate rows in place, just call this function with a placeholder field in that row. Use WordFile.AppendTableRow to add new row to the end or WordFile.DuplicateTableRow to add it after the referenced one.

parameters.name: WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

parameters.name: Placeholder
parameters.description: The name of the placeholder.

functions.name: WordFile.FieldNames
functions.description: Queries field names in the file. This looks for form fields in a word file.

parameters.name: WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

functions.name: WordFile.GetCaseless
functions.description: Queries caseless state.

parameters.name: WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

functions.name: WordFile.GetFieldText
functions.description: Queries field value. The plugin looks for the field with given name and queries it's text. Returns true if field was found.

parameters.name: WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

parameters.name: FieldName
parameters.description: The name of the field.

functions.name: WordFile.GetMediaFile

parameters.name: WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the item to query.

examples.label: Query custom properties of a word file:
examples.text_code:
#  open word file
Set Variable [
$wordfile
; Value:
MBS
( "
WordFile.OpenContainer
";
WordFile::Input
) ]
If [
MBS
("ISError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error" ;
$wordFile
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
#
#  read xml
Set Variable [
$xml
; Value:
MBS
( "WordFile.GetXML";
$WordFile
; "custom") ]
#
# get property count
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
( "
XML.NodeCount
";
$xml
; "property" ) ]
#
If [
$count
> 0 ]
Set Variable [
$i
; Value: 0 ]
#
Loop
Set Variable [
$name
; Value:
MBS
( "
XML.GetPathValue
";
$xml
; "Properties.property[" &
$i
& "]#name"; 1+2 ) ]
Set Variable [
$value
; Value:
MBS
( "
XML.GetPathValue
";
$xml
; "Properties.property[" &
$i
& "].lpwstr"; 1+2 ) ]
#
Show Custom Dialog [ "Custom propety" ;
$name
& ": " &
$value
]
#
Set Variable [
$i
; Value:
$i
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$i
>=
$count
]
End Loop
#
#
End If
#
#  free memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WordFile.Release
";
$WordFile
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>  open word file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WordFile.OpenContainer"; WordFile::Input ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$wordfile</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("ISError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Error" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$wordFile </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>  read xml</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WordFile.GetXML"; $WordFile; "custom") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$xml</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get property count</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.NodeCount"; $xml; "property" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $count > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$i</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.GetPathValue"; $xml; "Properties.property[" & $i & "]#name"; 1+2 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.GetPathValue"; $xml; "Properties.property[" & $i & "].lpwstr"; 1+2 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$value</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Custom propety" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$name & ": " & $value </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$i + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$i</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $i >= $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>  free memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WordFile.Release"; $WordFile) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

So please keep all text between and including the « and » markers (or whatever is configured) within the same font style.

Extract text from Word file::WordFile

$w

Extract text from Word file::Text
;

WordFile.Texts

WordFile.Release
";
$w

functions.name: WordFile.OpenFile
functions.description: Opens a word file from file. Can be a docx or a xml file. Reference numbers for WordFiles start at 46000. Does not work for old .doc files! May break if newer versions of Microsoft Word or Office change the docx file format. May also work for docm files. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path

examples.label: Opens file:

Let([
wordfile =

r1 =

WordFile.Substitute

r2 =

WordFile.WriteFile

r3 =

]; r1 & r2 )
Example result:
OKOK

functions.name: WordFile.RemoveTableRow
functions.description: Removes a table row containing a placeholder. If you have tables in your template word file and you only fill some rows, you can remove superfluous rows using this function.

parameters.name: WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

parameters.name: Placeholder
parameters.description: The name of the placeholder.

examples.label: Remove extra rows
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:5]
Loop
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WordFile.RemoveTableRow";
$WordFile
; "Bst_Menge_" & If(
$index
< 10; "0"; "") &
$index
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WordFile.RemoveTableRow";
$WordFile
; "Bst_Bemerkung_" & If(
$index
< 10; "0"; "") &
$index
)]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1]
Exit Loop If [
$index
> 20]
End Loop
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>5</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WordFile.RemoveTableRow"; $WordFile; "Bst_Menge_" & If($index < 10; "0"; "") & $index)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WordFile.RemoveTableRow"; $WordFile; "Bst_Bemerkung_" & If($index < 10; "0"; "") & $index)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>$index > 20</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WordFile.ReplaceTag
functions.description: Replaces a placeholder with text. The place holder in the document must start with « and end with » characters. The place holder you pass here, should not have those quotes. If you need to replace multi line text like addresses, please use several placeholders, at least one per line. Or for newer plugin pass text with new lines, but that will only work for main text and not headers. Version 7.0 and newer can handle multi line text better for normal paragraphs. It creates for you new paragraphs for each line in the new text. If you change font or style of the text within the marker, it will not be recognized. So please keep all text between and including the « and » markers (or whatever is configured) within the same font style.

parameters.name: WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

parameters.name: Placeholder
parameters.description: The name of the placeholder. In plugin version 7.4 and newer case insensitive for ASCII characters.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The new text.

parameters.name: All
parameters.description: Pass 1 to replace all placeholders with same text.

examples.label: Replaces placeholder with text:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WordFile.ReplaceTag";
$WordFile
; "City"; "Nickenich")]

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
