parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

parameters.name: FieldName
parameters.description: The name of the field.

parameters.name: FieldText
parameters.description: The new field text. Multi line text doesn't work.

examples.label: Set a field value:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WordFile.SetFieldText";
$WordFile
; "subject"; "Hello World" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WordFile.SetFieldText"; $WordFile; "subject"; "Hello World" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WordFile.SetMarkers
functions.description: Sets the markers to look for before/after a tag. Default are « and ». Should be different and not empty. Returns 1 on success or 0 on failure. Please don't use < or > for markers as those conflict with XML structure.

parameters.name: WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

parameters.name: OpenMarker
parameters.description: The new marker to search before tag.

parameters.name: CloseMarker
parameters.description: The new end marker.

functions.name: WordFile.SetMediaFile
functions.description: Sets a media file image. You can use this function to replace a placeholder image with a new image. Please make sure the new image has same dimensions and same file type as the original one.

parameters.name: WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the item to change.

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description: The container value with the image.

examples.label: Replace image:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$w
; Value:
MBS
( "
WordFile.OpenContainer
";
WordTest::wordfile
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WordFile.SetMediaFile";
$w
; "image1.jpeg";
WordTest::image
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WordFile.WriteFile
";
$w
; "/Users/cs/Desktop/output.docx")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WordFile.Release
";
$w
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WordFile.OpenContainer"; WordTest::wordfile )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$w</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WordFile.SetMediaFile"; $w; "image1.jpeg"; WordTest::image)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WordFile.WriteFile"; $w; "/Users/cs/Desktop/output.docx")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WordFile.Release"; $w)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WordFile.SetProperty
functions.description: Sets a metadata property. Updates the core properties for the document.

parameters.name: WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

parameters.name: FieldName
parameters.description: The name of the field. Can be title, subject, creator, keywords, description, lastModifiedBy, revision, created or modified.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new value.

examples.label: Set creator field:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$c
; Value:
MBS
( "WordFile.SetProperty";
$w
; "creator"; "Joe" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WordFile.SetProperty"; $w; "creator"; "Joe" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$c</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WordFile.SetSkipDeletedText
functions.description: Sets whether to skip deleted text. If the word file contains changes, it may log what text was deleted. We skip deleted text by default.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to skip deleted text or 0 to include deleted text.

functions.name: WordFile.SetXML
functions.description: Sets the xml for this word file.

parameters.name: WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The XML content for the word file.

parameters.name: Part
parameters.description: The name of the part to query. See WordFile.Parts for a list of parts. if no part is given, we use the document.

examples.label: Change XML directly:
examples.text_code:
# get xml
Set Variable [
$xml
; Value:
MBS
( "
WordFile.GetXML
";
$wordfile
) ]
# Change something
Set Variable [
$xml
; Value: Substitute(
$xml
; "Times New Roman"; "Helvetica") ]
# put xml back
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WordFile.SetXML";
$wordfile
;
$xml
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get xml</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WordFile.GetXML"; $wordfile) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$xml</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Change something</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Substitute($xml; "Times New Roman"; "Helvetica") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$xml</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> put xml back</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WordFile.SetXML"; $wordfile; $xml) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WordFile.Substitute
functions.description: Replaces some text in the document with new text. Like WordFile.ReplaceTag function, but without markers around the tag. Handles multi line text better for normal paragraphs. It creates for you new paragraphs for each line in the new text. If you change font or style of the text within the find text, it will not be recognized. So please keep all text within the same font style.

parameters.name: WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

parameters.name: FindText
parameters.description: The text to look for.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The new text.

parameters.name: All
parameters.description: Pass 1 to replace all occurrences with same text.

examples.label: Substitute a text in word file:
examples.text_code:
# load it
Set Variable [
$Wordfile
; Value:
MBS
( "
WordFile.OpenContainer
";
myTable::InputWordFile
) ]
# replace something
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WordFile.Substitute";
$Wordfile
;
$SearchWord
;
$ReplaceWord
]
# write it
Set Field [
myTable::OutputWordFile
; Value:
MBS
( "
WordFile.WriteContainer
";
$Wordfile
; "Test.docx" ) ]
# release memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
WordFile.Release
";
$Wordfile
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> load it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WordFile.OpenContainer"; myTable::InputWordFile ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Wordfile</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> replace something</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WordFile.Substitute"; $Wordfile; $SearchWord; $ReplaceWord  </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> write it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>Value: MBS( "WordFile.WriteContainer"; $Wordfile; "Test.docx" ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="myTable" id="99999" name="OutputWordFile "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> release memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WordFile.Release"; $Wordfile) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WordFile.Tags
functions.description: Queries list of tags. Depends on the start/end markers set with WordFile.SetMarkers function. Can be very useful for debugging to query list of tags found in the document.

parameters.name: WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

functions.name: WordFile.Texts
functions.description: Extracts text of word file. Plugin inserts a few newline characters for detected line ends. May not give all text. If you miss something, please send us test files.

parameters.name: WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

examples.label: Extracts text:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("WordFile.Texts"; $WordFile)</Calculation>
    <Field table="WordFiles" id="99999" name="Texts"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Open word file and extract text:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$w
; Value:
MBS
( "
WordFile.OpenContainer
";
Extract text from Word file::WordFile
)]
If [
MBS
("iserror")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Failed to read word file";
$w
]
Exit Script []
End If
Set Field [
Extract text from Word file::Text
;
MBS
( "WordFile.Texts";
$w
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WordFile.Release
";
$w
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WordFile.OpenContainer"; Extract text from Word file::WordFile )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$w</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("iserror")</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Failed to read word file"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$w</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "WordFile.Texts"; $w )</Calculation>
    <Field table="Extract text from Word file" id="99999" name="Text"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WordFile.Release"; $w )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.name: WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name to use for the container.

examples.label: Substitute a text in word file:

$Wordfile
; Value:

";
myTable::InputWordFile
) ]

( "
WordFile.Substitute
";
$Wordfile
;
$SearchWord
;
$ReplaceWord
]
# write it
Set Field [
myTable::OutputWordFile
; Value:
MBS
( "WordFile.WriteContainer";
$Wordfile
; "Test.docx" ) ]
# release memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
WordFile.Release
";
$Wordfile
) ]

functions.name: WordFile.WriteFile
functions.description: Writes a file to disk. File extension should be correct, either docx or xml depending on what you loaded. May break if newer versions of Microsoft Word or Office change the docx file format. See FileDialog functions if you like to show a save dialog to pick a destination for the file path. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: WordFile
parameters.description: The reference number of the open word file.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path.

examples.label: Writes a DocX file:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WordFile.WriteFile";
$WordFile
; "/Users/cs/Desktop/TestOutput.docx")]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

      <Calculation>MBS("WordFile.WriteFile"; $WordFile; "/Users/cs/Desktop/TestOutput.docx")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>
